WITH RECURSIVE 
-- First, normalize the data: create a table of (user_id, ref) pairs
user_refs AS (
    SELECT id, username, ref1 AS ref FROM users WHERE ref1 IS NOT NULL
    UNION ALL
    SELECT id, username, ref2 AS ref FROM users WHERE ref2 IS NOT NULL
),
-- Find all connected users using recursive traversal
connected AS (
    -- Base case: each user starts as their own component with a canonical id (min id in group)
    SELECT 
        id AS user_id,
        id AS root_id,
        id AS canonical_id,
        username,
        ref1,
        ref2
    FROM users
    UNION ALL
    -- Recursive case: find users connected through shared refs
    SELECT 
        u.id AS user_id,
        c.root_id,
        LEAST(c.canonical_id, u.id) AS canonical_id,
        u.username,
        u.ref1,
        u.ref2
    FROM users u
    JOIN user_refs ur1 ON u.id = ur1.id
    JOIN user_refs ur2 ON ur1.ref = ur2.ref AND ur1.id != ur2.id
    JOIN connected c ON ur2.id = c.user_id
    WHERE u.id > c.user_id  -- Prevent cycles
),
-- Get the final canonical grouping for each user
final_groups AS (
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        MIN(canonical_id) AS group_id
    FROM connected
    GROUP BY user_id
),
-- Aggregate user info by group
grouped_users AS (
    SELECT 
        fg.group_id,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT u.id ORDER BY u.id) AS ids,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT u.username ORDER BY u.username) AS usernames,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT u.ref1 ORDER BY u.ref1) FILTER (WHERE u.ref1 IS NOT NULL) AS refs1,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT u.ref2 ORDER BY u.ref2) FILTER (WHERE u.ref2 IS NOT NULL) AS refs2
    FROM final_groups fg
    JOIN users u ON fg.user_id = u.id
    GROUP BY fg.group_id
),
-- Handle assets with matching users
assets_with_users AS (
    SELECT 
        gu.group_id,
        gu.ids,
        gu.usernames,
        gu.refs1,
        gu.refs2,
        COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) AS asset_count,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT a.id ORDER BY a.id) AS asset_ids
    FROM grouped_users gu
    LEFT JOIN assets a ON a.owner = ANY(gu.refs1) OR a.owner = ANY(gu.refs2)
    GROUP BY gu.group_id, gu.ids, gu.usernames, gu.refs1, gu.refs2
),
-- Handle assets with no matching user (propagate owner to ref2)
orphan_assets AS (
    SELECT 
        NULL::integer[] AS ids,
        NULL::text[] AS usernames,
        NULL::text[] AS refs1,
        ARRAY[a.owner] AS refs2,
        1 AS asset_count,
        ARRAY[a.id] AS asset_ids
    FROM assets a
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM users u 
        WHERE a.owner = u.ref1 OR a.owner = u.ref2
    )
)
SELECT ids, usernames, refs1, refs2, asset_count, asset_ids 
FROM assets_with_users
WHERE asset_count > 0
UNION ALL
SELECT ids, usernames, refs1, refs2, asset_count, asset_ids 
FROM orphan_assets
ORDER BY COALESCE(ids[1], 0), asset_ids[1]

WITH attendance_count AS (
    SELECT link_to_event, COUNT(link_to_member) AS attendee_count
    FROM attendance
    GROUP BY link_to_event
),
budget_count AS (
    SELECT link_to_event, COUNT(*) AS budget_record_count
    FROM budget
    GROUP BY link_to_event
)
SELECT 
    a.link_to_event,
    a.attendee_count,
    b.budget_record_count
FROM attendance_count a
LEFT JOIN budget_count b ON a.link_to_event = b.link_to_event
WHERE b.link_to_event IS NULL OR a.attendee_count != b.budget_record_count

UNION ALL

SELECT 
    b.link_to_event,
    a.attendee_count,
    b.budget_record_count
FROM budget_count b
LEFT JOIN attendance_count a ON a.link_to_event = b.link_to_event
WHERE a.link_to_event IS NULL

)

SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS count,
    MIN(releaseDate) AS start_date,
    MAX(releaseDate) AS end_date
FROM (
    SELECT 
        releaseDate,
        releaseDate - (ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY releaseDate) * INTERVAL '1 day') AS grp
    FROM (
        SELECT DISTINCT releaseDate 
        FROM sets_releaseInfo
    ) t
) t1
GROUP BY grp
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY count DESC

WITH RECURSIVE path_cte AS (
  -- Start from cards that have no previous card (not referenced by any nextCardId)
  -- These are the true starting points of sequences
  SELECT 
    c.id, 
    c.nextCardId, 
    c.id::TEXT AS Path,
    c.id AS start_id
  FROM collection c
  WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM collection c2 WHERE c2.nextCardId = c.id
  )
  
  UNION ALL
  
  -- Build path forward
  SELECT 
    c.id, 
    c.nextCardId, 
    p.Path || ' --> ' || c.id,
    p.start_id
  FROM collection c
  JOIN path_cte p ON c.id = p.nextCardId
)
-- Only select paths that end at cards with no next card (complete paths)
SELECT Path
FROM path_cte
WHERE nextCardId IS NULL
ORDER BY start_id

SELECT 
    (SELECT r.relname FROM pg_class r WHERE r.oid = c.confrelid) AS base_table,
    a.attname AS base_col,
    (SELECT r.relname FROM pg_class r WHERE r.oid = c.conrelid) AS referencing_table,
    ref_a.attname AS referencing_col,
    pg_get_constraintdef(c.oid) AS constraint_sql
FROM pg_constraint c
JOIN pg_attribute a ON c.confrelid = a.attrelid AND a.attnum = ANY(c.confkey)
JOIN LATERAL UNNEST(c.conkey) AS ref_attnum ON true
JOIN pg_attribute ref_a ON c.conrelid = ref_a.attrelid AND ref_a.attnum = ref_attnum
WHERE c.confrelid = (SELECT oid FROM pg_class WHERE relname = 'users')
  AND c.confrelid != c.conrelid
  AND c.contype = 'f'

WITH monthly_purchases AS (
    -- Get distinct months per user
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        user_id,
        DATE_TRUNC('month', TO_DATE(purchase_date || '-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD')) AS month_date
    FROM purchase
),
streaks AS (
    -- Calculate streak groups using the difference between month_date and row_number
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        month_date,
        month_date - INTERVAL '1 month' * ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY month_date) AS grp
    FROM monthly_purchases
),
streak_lengths AS (
    -- Calculate length of each streak
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        grp,
        COUNT(*) AS streak_length
    FROM streaks
    GROUP BY user_id, grp
),
max_streaks AS (
    -- Get the longest streak for each user
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        MAX(streak_length) AS longest_streak
    FROM streak_lengths
    GROUP BY user_id
)
-- Count how many users have each longest streak length
SELECT 
    longest_streak AS streak_length,
    COUNT(*) AS num_users
FROM max_streaks
GROUP BY longest_streak
ORDER BY longest_streak

SELECT DISTINCT user_id, email 
FROM orders 
WHERE user_id IN (
    -- Condition 1: luxury segment with New York destination
    SELECT user_id FROM orders WHERE segment = 'luxury' AND destination = 'New York'
    INTERSECT
    -- Condition 2: luxury segment with London destination  
    SELECT user_id FROM orders WHERE segment = 'luxury' AND destination = 'London'
    INTERSECT
    -- Condition 3: basic segment with New York destination and total revenue > 2000
    SELECT user_id FROM orders 
    WHERE segment = 'basic' AND destination = 'New York' 
    GROUP BY user_id 
    HAVING SUM(revenue) > 2000
    INTERSECT
    -- Condition 4: never made a purchase with Miami destination
    SELECT user_id FROM orders 
    WHERE user_id NOT IN (SELECT user_id FROM orders WHERE destination = 'Miami')
)

    COALESCE((regexp_match(version, '(\d+)\.(\d+)\.(\d+)-?(\d*)'))[4], '0')::int DESC

SELECT 
    alignment_totals.alignment,
    alignment_totals.total_by_alignment,
    R.race,
    COUNT(*) AS count_by_alignment_race
FROM superhero S
JOIN alignment A ON S.alignment_id = A.id
JOIN race R ON S.race_id = R.id
JOIN (
    SELECT 
        A.alignment,
        COUNT(*) AS total_by_alignment
    FROM superhero S
    JOIN alignment A ON S.alignment_id = A.id
    GROUP BY A.alignment
) alignment_totals ON A.alignment = alignment_totals.alignment
GROUP BY A.alignment, R.race, alignment_totals.total_by_alignment

SELECT 
    label,
    JSON_AGG(
        JSON_BUILD_OBJECT(
            'molecule_id', m.molecule_id,
            'atoms', COALESCE(molecule_atoms.atoms, '[]'::json),
            'bonds', COALESCE(molecule_bonds.bonds, '[]'::json)
        )
    ) AS groupedMolecules
FROM molecule m
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT 
        molecule_id,
        JSON_AGG(JSON_BUILD_OBJECT('atom_id', atom_id, 'element', element)) AS atoms
    FROM atom
    GROUP BY molecule_id
) molecule_atoms ON m.molecule_id = molecule_atoms.molecule_id
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT 
        molecule_id,
        JSON_AGG(JSON_BUILD_OBJECT('bond_id', bond_id, 'bond_type', bond_type)) AS bonds
    FROM bond
    GROUP BY molecule_id
) molecule_bonds ON m.molecule_id = molecule_bonds.molecule_id
GROUP BY label

SELECT

    MAX(CASE WHEN element = 'c' OR element = 'cl' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS c_or_cl_atoms 
FROM atom 
GROUP BY molecule_id

WITH signed_transactions AS (
    SELECT 
        transaction_date,
        club_name,
        amount,
        CASE WHEN amount >= 0 THEN 1 ELSE -1 END AS sign_val
    FROM club_transactions
),
grouped AS (
    SELECT 
        transaction_date,
        club_name,
        amount,
        sign_val,
        SUM(CASE WHEN sign_val != LAG(sign_val) OVER (PARTITION BY club_name ORDER BY transaction_date) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) 
            OVER (PARTITION BY club_name ORDER BY transaction_date) AS grp
    FROM signed_transactions
)
SELECT 
    MIN(transaction_date) AS start_date,
    MAX(transaction_date) AS end_date,
    club_name,
    SUM(amount) AS total_amount,
    CASE WHEN MAX(sign_val) = 1 THEN 'income' ELSE 'expense' END AS transaction_type
FROM grouped
GROUP BY club_name, grp
ORDER BY club_name, start_date

WITH RECURSIVE module_tree AS (
    -- Base case: get all parent modules (id_parent_module is NULL or not found in id_module)
    SELECT 
        m.id_module,
        m.id_parent_module,
        m.module_code,
        m.name_module,
        m.desc_module,
        0 AS level
    FROM modules m
    WHERE m.id_parent_module IS NULL OR m.id_parent_module NOT IN (SELECT id_module FROM modules)
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: get child modules
    SELECT 
        m.id_module,
        m.id_parent_module,
        m.module_code,
        m.name_module,
        m.desc_module,
        mt.level + 1
    FROM modules m
    INNER JOIN module_tree mt ON m.id_parent_module = mt.id_module
),
-- Get all parent modules with their children as JSON array
parent_modules AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT
        mt.id_module,
        mt.id_parent_module,
        mt.module_code,
        mt.name_module,
        mt.desc_module
    FROM module_tree mt
    WHERE mt.level = 0
)
SELECT json_agg(
    json_build_object(
        'id_module', pm.id_module,
        'desc_module', pm.desc_module,
        'module_code', pm.module_code,
        'name_module', pm.name_module,
        'id_parent_module', pm.id_parent_module,
        'children', (
            SELECT COALESCE(json_agg(
                json_build_object(
                    'id_module', c.id_module,
                    'desc_module', c.desc_module,
                    'module_code', c.module_code,
                    'name_module', c.name_module,
                    'id_parent_module', c.id_parent_module
                ) ORDER BY c.id_module DESC
            ), '[]'::json)
            FROM module_tree c
            WHERE c.id_parent_module = pm.id_module AND c.level > 0
        )
    ) ORDER BY pm.id_module
)
FROM parent_modules pm

WITH input_rows(customerid, segment, currency, row_idx) AS (
    SELECT *, row_number() OVER ()
    FROM (VALUES (3, 'SME', 'EUR'), (1, 'KAM', 'CZK'), (3, 'SME', 'EUR')) 
    AS t(customerid, segment, currency)
),
deduped AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT ON (customerid) customerid, segment, currency
    FROM input_rows
    ORDER BY customerid
),
inserted AS (
    INSERT INTO customers (customerid, segment, currency)
    SELECT customerid, segment, currency FROM deduped
    ON CONFLICT (customerid) DO UPDATE 
    SET segment = EXCLUDED.segment,
        currency = EXCLUDED.currency
    RETURNING customerid
)
SELECT i.customerid 
FROM input_rows i
JOIN inserted ins ON ins.customerid = i.customerid
ORDER BY i.row_idx

    CASE 
        WHEN jsonb_typeof(disp.addresses) = 'array' THEN disp.addresses 
        ELSE '[]'::jsonb 
    END
) AS address 
WHERE disp.client_id IN (100, 414)

DELETE FROM cards
WHERE ctid IN (
    SELECT ctid
    FROM cards c
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM norm1 n1
        WHERE n1.uuid = c.uuid
        AND n1.setcode = c.setcode
        AND n1.rarity = c.rarity
        AND n1.manacost = c.manacost
        AND n1.uuid IS NOT NULL
        AND n1.setcode IS NOT NULL
        AND n1.rarity IS NOT NULL
        AND n1.manacost IS NOT NULL
    )
    AND NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM norm2 n2
        WHERE n2.uuid = c.uuid
        AND n2.setcode = c.setcode
        AND n2.rarity = c.rarity
        AND n2.manacost = c.manacost
        AND n2.uuid IS NOT NULL
        AND n2.setcode IS NOT NULL
        AND n2.rarity IS NOT NULL
        AND n2.manacost IS NOT NULL
    )

RETURNS TABLE (month_date date) 
LANGUAGE SQL 
IMMUTABLE

WITH match_with_groups AS (
    SELECT 
        m.id,
        m.date,
        m.home_team_goal,
        m.away_team_goal,
        CASE WHEN m.home_team_goal = m.away_team_goal THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_draw,
        SUM(CASE WHEN m.home_team_goal = m.away_team_goal THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
            OVER (ORDER BY m.date, m.id) AS draw_group
    FROM match AS m
),
running_totals AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        date,
        home_team_goal,
        away_team_goal,
        is_draw,
        draw_group,
        SUM(CASE WHEN is_draw = 0 THEN home_team_goal + away_team_goal ELSE 0 END)
            OVER (PARTITION BY draw_group ORDER BY date, id) AS amount
    FROM match_with_groups
)
SELECT 
    id,
    date,
    1 AS invoiced,
    amount
FROM running_totals
WHERE is_draw = 1
ORDER BY date, id

WITH LastTransactionPerStation AS (
  -- Get the last transaction at each gas station for each customer
  SELECT
    *,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customerid, gasstationid ORDER BY date DESC, time DESC) as rn_per_station
  FROM transactions_1k
),
LastTransactions AS (
  -- Keep only the last transaction per gas station
  SELECT *
  FROM LastTransactionPerStation
  WHERE rn_per_station = 1
),
CustomerIssues AS (
  -- Find the first gas station (chronologically) where amount < 10 occurred
  SELECT DISTINCT
    customerid,
    FIRST_VALUE(gasstationid) OVER (
      PARTITION BY customerid 
      ORDER BY date, time
      ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING
    ) as first_issue_gasstation
  FROM LastTransactions
  WHERE amount < 10
),
RankedStations AS (
  -- Rank all last-transactions-per-station by date/time for each customer
  SELECT
    lt.*,
    ci.first_issue_gasstation,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY lt.customerid ORDER BY lt.date DESC, lt.time DESC) as rn_last,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY lt.customerid ORDER BY lt.date, lt.time) as rn_first
  FROM LastTransactions lt
  LEFT JOIN CustomerIssues ci ON lt.customerid = ci.customerid
)
SELECT
  customerid,
  transactionid,
  gasstationid,
  amount
FROM RankedStations
WHERE
  -- If there's an issue (amount < 10 somewhere), show the first station where it occurred
  (first_issue_gasstation IS NOT NULL AND gasstationid = first_issue_gasstation)
  -- If no issue, show the last station with amount >= 10
  OR (first_issue_gasstation IS NULL AND rn_last = 1 AND amount >= 10)

SELECT c1.user_id, c1.action_time as search_time
FROM user_actions c1
JOIN LATERAL (
    SELECT MIN(action_time) as next_login_time
    FROM user_actions c2
    WHERE c2.user_id = c1.user_id
    AND c2.action = 'LOGIN'
    AND c2.action_time > c1.action_time
) next_login ON next_login.next_login_time IS NOT NULL
WHERE c1.action = 'SEARCH'
AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM user_actions c0 
    WHERE c0.user_id = c1.user_id 
    AND c0.action = 'LOGIN' 
    AND c0.action_time < c1.action_time
)
AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM user_actions c3
    WHERE c3.user_id = c1.user_id
    AND c3.action = 'BUY'
    AND c3.action_time > c1.action_time
    AND c3.action_time < next_login.next_login_time
)

SELECT 
    begin_date,
    end_date,
    GREATEST(0,
        (EXTRACT(YEAR FROM last_month_plus_one) - EXTRACT(YEAR FROM first_month)) * 12 +
        (EXTRACT(MONTH FROM last_month_plus_one) - EXTRACT(MONTH FROM first_month))
    )::integer AS full_calendar_months
FROM (
    SELECT 
        begin_date,
        end_date,
        -- First day of first full month
        CASE 
            WHEN begin_date = date_trunc('month', begin_date) 
            THEN begin_date 
            ELSE date_trunc('month', begin_date) + interval '1 month' 
        END AS first_month,
        -- Day after last day of last full month
        date_trunc('month', end_date) + 
            CASE 
                WHEN end_date = date_trunc('month', end_date) + interval '1 month - 1 day' 
                THEN interval '1 month' 
                ELSE interval '0 month' 
            END AS last_month_plus_one
    FROM race_dates
) t

WITH changes AS (
    SELECT 
        entity_id, 
        json_object_agg(column_id, value) as change, 
        updated_at 
    FROM player_stats_changes 
    GROUP BY entity_id, updated_at
),
expanded AS (
    SELECT 
        entity_id,
        (change->>'height')::numeric as height_val,
        (change->>'weight')::numeric as weight_val,
        updated_at
    FROM changes
),
grouped AS (
    SELECT 
        entity_id,
        height_val,
        weight_val,
        updated_at,
        COUNT(height_val) OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id ORDER BY updated_at) as height_grp,
        COUNT(weight_val) OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id ORDER BY updated_at) as weight_grp
    FROM expanded
)
SELECT 
    entity_id,
    MAX(height_val) OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id, height_grp) as height,
    MAX(weight_val) OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id, weight_grp) as weight,
    updated_at
FROM grouped
ORDER BY entity_id, updated_at

WITH RECURSIVE 
-- Get all items with their subgroups
qr1(item, subgroup) AS (
    SELECT id, subgroup1 
    FROM superhero_group 
    WHERE subgroup1 IS NOT NULL
), 
qr2(item, subgroup) AS (
    SELECT id, subgroup2 
    FROM superhero_group 
    WHERE subgroup2 IS NOT NULL
),
-- Combine all subgroup relationships (both directions)
all_relations AS (
    SELECT item, subgroup FROM qr1
    UNION
    SELECT item, subgroup FROM qr2
),
-- Build edges: items are connected if they share a subgroup
edges AS (
    SELECT a.item AS item1, b.item AS item2
    FROM all_relations a
    JOIN all_relations b ON a.subgroup = b.subgroup AND a.item != b.item
),
-- Get all unique items
all_items AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT item FROM all_relations
),
-- Recursive CTE to find connected components
connected AS (
    -- Base case: each item starts as its own component (using min item as representative)
    SELECT item, item AS component
    FROM all_items
    
    UNION
    
    -- Recursive case: follow edges to find connected items
    SELECT e.item2, c.component
    FROM connected c
    JOIN edges e ON c.item = e.item1
    WHERE e.item2 < c.component  -- Prevent cycles, keep minimum as component
),
-- Get final component assignment (minimum item in each connected component)
final_components AS (
    SELECT item, MIN(component) AS component
    FROM connected
    GROUP BY item
)
-- Final output with dense_rank for clean group IDs
SELECT 
    fc.item,
    qr1.subgroup AS subgroup1,
    qr2.subgroup AS subgroup2,
    DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY fc.component) AS group_id
FROM final_components fc
LEFT JOIN qr1 ON fc.item = qr1.item
LEFT JOIN qr2 ON fc.item = qr2.item
ORDER BY fc.item

WITH all_aggregations AS (
    -- Full granularity
    SELECT id, date_of_visit, utm_campaign, utm_source, utm_medium, utm_content, utm_term,
           SUM(sessions) AS sessions, SUM(pageviews) AS pageviews, SUM(bounces) AS bounces,
           31 AS sig  -- 1+2+4+8+16
    FROM report
    GROUP BY id, date_of_visit, utm_campaign, utm_source, utm_medium, utm_content, utm_term
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Campaign + Source + Medium
    SELECT id, date_of_visit, utm_campaign, utm_source, utm_medium, NULL, NULL,
           SUM(sessions), SUM(pageviews), SUM(bounces), 7  -- 1+2+4
    FROM report
    GROUP BY id, date_of_visit, utm_campaign, utm_source, utm_medium
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Campaign + Source
    SELECT id, date_of_visit, utm_campaign, utm_source, NULL, NULL, NULL,
           SUM(sessions), SUM(pageviews), SUM(bounces), 3  -- 1+2
    FROM report
    GROUP BY id, date_of_visit, utm_campaign, utm_source
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Campaign only
    SELECT id, date_of_visit, utm_campaign, NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL,
           SUM(sessions), SUM(pageviews), SUM(bounces), 1
    FROM report
    GROUP BY id, date_of_visit, utm_campaign
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Source + Medium
    SELECT id, date_of_visit, NULL, utm_source, utm_medium, NULL, NULL,
           SUM(sessions), SUM(pageviews), SUM(bounces), 6  -- 2+4
    FROM report
    GROUP BY id, date_of_visit, utm_source, utm_medium
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Source only
    SELECT id, date_of_visit, NULL, utm_source, NULL, NULL, NULL,
           SUM(sessions), SUM(pageviews), SUM(bounces), 2
    FROM report
    GROUP BY id, date_of_visit, utm_source
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Medium only
    SELECT id, date_of_visit, NULL, NULL, utm_medium, NULL, NULL,
           SUM(sessions), SUM(pageviews), SUM(bounces), 4
    FROM report
    GROUP BY id, date_of_visit, utm_medium
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- No UTM params (id + date only)
    SELECT id, date_of_visit, NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL,
           SUM(sessions), SUM(pageviews), SUM(bounces), 0
    FROM report
    GROUP BY id, date_of_visit
),
utm_sig AS (
    SELECT row_id, utm_campaign, utm_source, utm_medium, utm_content, utm_term,
           (CASE WHEN utm_campaign IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END +
            CASE WHEN utm_source IS NOT NULL THEN 2 ELSE 0 END +
            CASE WHEN utm_medium IS NOT NULL THEN 4 ELSE 0 END +
            CASE WHEN utm_content IS NOT NULL THEN 8 ELSE 0 END +
            CASE WHEN utm_term IS NOT NULL THEN 16 ELSE 0 END) AS sig
    FROM utm
)
SELECT u.row_id, u.utm_campaign, u.utm_source, u.utm_medium, u.utm_content, u.utm_term,
       a.date_of_visit, a.sessions, a.pageviews, a.bounces
FROM utm_sig u
LEFT JOIN all_aggregations a ON a.id = u.row_id
    AND (u.utm_campaign IS NULL OR a.utm_campaign = u.utm_campaign)
    AND (u.utm_source IS NULL OR a.utm_source = u.utm_source)
    AND (u.utm_medium IS NULL OR a.utm_medium = u.utm_medium)
    AND (u.utm_content IS NULL OR a.utm_content = u.utm_content)
    AND (u.utm_term IS NULL OR a.utm_term = u.utm_term)
    AND a.sig = u.sig

WITH race_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        gender_id,
        race_id,
        COUNT(*) as superhero_count
    FROM superhero
    GROUP BY gender_id, race_id
),
ranked_races AS (
    SELECT 
        gender_id,
        race_id,
        superhero_count,
        DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY gender_id ORDER BY superhero_count DESC) as top_rank,
        DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY gender_id ORDER BY superhero_count ASC) as bottom_rank
    FROM race_counts
)
SELECT 
    gender_id,
    race_id,
    superhero_count,
    CASE 
        WHEN top_rank <= 5 THEN 'top'
        WHEN bottom_rank <= 5 THEN 'bottom'
    END as rank_type
FROM ranked_races
WHERE top_rank <= 5 OR bottom_rank <= 5
ORDER BY gender_id,

-- Test on example table with ffill on 'val' column
WITH example_ordered AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        str,
        val,
        row_number() OVER (ORDER BY created_at, id) as rn
    FROM example
),
filled AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        str,
        val,
        -- Forward fill: get last non-null value
        (array_agg(val) FILTER (WHERE val IS NOT NULL) 
         OVER (ORDER BY rn ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW))[array_length(array_agg(val) FILTER (WHERE val IS NOT NULL) OVER (ORDER BY rn ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW), 1)] as val_filled
    FROM example_ordered
)
SELECT id, str, val, val_filled
FROM filled
ORDER BY rn

SELECT 
    SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp::date = '2022-01-27'::date - 1 THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS yesterday_revenue,
    ROUND(
        (SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp::date = '2022-01-27'::date - 1 THEN amount ELSE 0 END) - 
         SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp::date = '2022-01-27'::date - 8 THEN amount ELSE 0 END)) * 1.0 / 
        NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp::date = '2022-01-27'::date - 8 THEN amount ELSE 0 END), 0), 
        2
    ) AS pct_change_week_ago,
    SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp >= date_trunc('month', '2022-01-27'::date - 1) 
              AND timestamp::date <= '2022-01-27'::date - 1 THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS mtd,
    ROUND(
        (SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp >= date_trunc('month', '2022-01-27'::date - 1) 
                  AND timestamp::date <= '2022-01-27'::date - 1 THEN amount ELSE 0 END) - 
         SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp >= date_trunc('month', '2022-01-27'::date - 1) - interval '1 month' 
                  AND timestamp::date <= '2022-01-27'::date - 1 - interval '1 month' THEN amount ELSE 0 END)) * 1.0 /
        NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp >= date_trunc('month', '2022-01-27'::date - 1) - interval '1 month' 
                        AND timestamp::date <= '2022-01-27'::date - 1 - interval '1 month' THEN amount ELSE 0 END), 0),
        2
    ) AS pct_change_month_prior 
FROM transactions

SELECT p.id, p.acceptedanswerid, p.posttypeid, p.score 
FROM posts p
WHERE 
    -- Case 1: Parent post with score >= 20
    (p.acceptedanswerid IS NULL AND p.score >= 20)
    OR
    -- Case 2: Child post where parent score < 20 but child score >= 20
    (p.acceptedanswerid IS NOT NULL 
     AND p.score >= 20 
     AND EXISTS (
         SELECT 1 FROM posts parent 
         WHERE parent.id = p.acceptedanswerid 
         AND parent.score < 20
     ))

WITH card_activity AS (
    SELECT 
        to_date(fe.start_date_key::text, 'YYYYMMDD') AS start_date,
        to_date(fe.end_date_key::text, 'YYYYMMDD') AS end_date,
        dp.set_name,
        dp.set_code
    FROM fact_collection fe
    INNER JOIN dim_set dp ON fe.set_key = dp.set_key
),
months AS (
    SELECT generate_series(
        DATE '2023-01-01',
        '2100-12-01'::date,
        INTERVAL '1 month'
    )::date AS month_start
),
all_sets AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT set_name FROM card_activity
),
month_set_combinations AS (
    SELECT m.month_start, s.set_name
    FROM months m
    CROSS JOIN all_sets s
)
SELECT 
    msc.month_start AS month,
    msc.set_name,
    COUNT(sa.start_date) AS existing_cards
FROM month_set_combinations msc
LEFT JOIN card_activity sa 
    ON sa.set_name = msc.set_name
    AND sa.start_date < (msc.month_start + INTERVAL '1 month')::date
    AND (sa.end_date IS NULL OR sa.end_date >= msc.month_start)
GROUP BY msc.month_start, msc.set_name
ORDER BY msc.month_start, msc.set_name

WITH mission_spans AS (
    SELECT 
        hero_id, 
        mission_start, 
        mission_end,
        LAG(mission_end) OVER (PARTITION BY hero_id ORDER BY mission_start) AS prev_end
    FROM superhero_missions
),
grouped AS (
    SELECT 
        hero_id,
        mission_start,
        mission_end,
        SUM(CASE WHEN prev_end IS NULL OR prev_end != mission_start THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) 
            OVER (PARTITION BY hero_id ORDER BY mission_start) AS grp
    FROM mission_spans
)
SELECT 
    hero_id,
    MIN(mission_start) AS span_start,
    MAX(mission_end) AS span_end
FROM grouped
GROUP BY hero_id, grp
ORDER BY hero_id, span_start
SELECT percentile_cont(0.5) within group (ORDER by releasedate)::date FROM sets

UPDATE latest_trans lt
SET 
    amount = t.amount,
    trans_id = t.trans_id
FROM (
    SELECT DISTINCT ON (account_id) 
        account_id, 
        amount, 
        trans_id
    FROM trans
    ORDER BY account_id, trans_id DESC
) t
WHERE lt.account_id = t.account_id

WITH RECURSIVE related_leagues AS (
    -- Base case: Start with specific league
    SELECT 
        id as league_id,
        name,
        country_id
    FROM league
    WHERE id = 1
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: Find other leagues in same country
    SELECT 
        l.id,
        l.name,
        l.country_id
    FROM league l
    JOIN related_leagues rl ON l.country_id = rl.country_id 
        AND l.id > rl.league_id  -- Ensure progress to avoid duplicates
)
SELECT DISTINCT
    rl.league_id,
    rl.name as league_name,
    t.team_api_id,
    t.team_long_name as team_name
FROM related_leagues rl
JOIN match m ON m.league_id = rl.league_id
JOIN team t ON t.team_api_id IN (m.home_team_api_id, m.away_team_api_id)
ORDER BY rl.league_id, t.team_long_name

WITH hero_attribute_data (hero_id, attribute_id, max_score, year_1, year_2, year_3, year_4, year_5) AS (
    VALUES 
        (1, 1, 80, 80, 80, 80, null, 80), 
        (2, 2, 90, 90, 85, 90, 88, 90), 
        (3, 3, 75, 75, 70, null, 75, 75), 
        (4, 4, 60, null, 60, 60, 60, null)
), 
score_check AS (
    SELECT *, 
        CASE 
            WHEN year_1 IS NULL OR year_1 <> max_score
              OR year_2 IS NULL OR year_2 <> max_score
              OR year_3 IS NULL OR year_3 <> max_score
              OR year_4 IS NULL OR year_4 <> max_score
              OR year_5 IS NULL OR year_5 <> max_score
            THEN true 
            ELSE false 
        END AS has_gap_or_change
    FROM hero_attribute_data
) 
SELECT * 
FROM score_check 
WHERE has_gap_or_change = true

WITH RECURSIVE craft_tree AS (
    -- Base case: start with all store entries
    SELECT 
        item_id,
        item_price as retail_price,
        uses_item_id,
        uses_item_price,
        resource_id,
        resource_price,
        quantity,
        1 as level,
        CAST(COALESCE(resource_price, 0) AS REAL) as accumulated_resource_cost,
        CAST(COALESCE(uses_item_price, 0) AS REAL) as accumulated_item_cost
    FROM store
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: follow item dependencies
    SELECT 
        ct.item_id,
        ct.retail_price,
        s.uses_item_id,
        s.uses_item_price,
        s.resource_id,
        s.resource_price,
        ct.quantity,
        ct.level + 1,
        ct.accumulated_resource_cost + COALESCE(s.resource_price, 0),
        ct.accumulated_item_cost + COALESCE(s.uses_item_price, 0)
    FROM craft_tree ct
    JOIN store s ON ct.uses_item_id = s.item_id
    WHERE ct.uses_item_id IS NOT NULL
),
-- Calculate partial craft price (resources only, buying required items)
partial_craft AS (
    SELECT 
        item_id,
        SUM(COALESCE(resource_price * quantity, 0)) as partial_craft_price
    FROM store
    GROUP BY item_id
),
-- Calculate full craft price (recursive - craft all dependencies)
full_craft AS (
    SELECT 
        item_id,
        MIN(accumulated_resource_cost + accumulated_item_cost) as full_craft_price
    FROM craft_tree
    WHERE uses_item_id IS NULL  -- leaf nodes (no more dependencies)
    GROUP BY item_id
)
SELECT 
    s.item_id,
    MAX(s.item_price) as retail_price,
    MAX(pc.partial_craft_price) as partial_craft_price,
    COALESCE(MAX(fc.full_craft_price), MAX(pc.partial_craft_price)) as full_craft_price,
    CASE 
        WHEN MAX(s.item_price) <= COALESCE(MAX(fc.full_craft_price), MAX(pc.partial_craft_price)) 
        THEN 'Buy' 
        ELSE 'Craft' 
    END as recommendation
FROM store s
LEFT JOIN partial_craft pc ON s.item_id = pc.item_id
LEFT JOIN full_craft fc ON s.item_id = fc.item_id
GROUP BY s.item_id

WITH keys_expanded AS (
    SELECT 
        customerid,
        key,
        value
    FROM card_preference,
    LATERAL jsonb_each(preference) AS j(key, value)
),
array_elements AS (
    SELECT 
        customerid,
        key,
        CASE 
            WHEN jsonb_typeof(value) = 'array' THEN elem
            ELSE value
        END AS elem
    FROM keys_expanded,
    LATERAL jsonb_array_elements(
        CASE 
            WHEN jsonb_typeof(value) = 'array' THEN value
            ELSE jsonb_build_array(value)
        END
    ) AS t(elem)
),
aggregated AS (
    SELECT 
        customerid,
        key,
        jsonb_agg(DISTINCT elem) AS unique_values
    FROM array_elements
    WHERE customerid = '123.abc'
    GROUP BY customerid, key
)
SELECT 
    customerid,
    jsonb_object_agg(key, unique_values) AS preferences
FROM aggregated
GROUP BY customerid

WITH event_days AS (
    -- Get distinct days for 'April Meeting' in MST timezone
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        DATE_TRUNC('day', event_date AT TIME ZONE 'UTC' AT TIME ZONE 'MST')::date AS event_day
    FROM event
    WHERE event_name = 'April Meeting'
),
numbered_days AS (
    -- Number the days and calculate a group identifier for streaks
    SELECT 
        event_day,
        event_day - (ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY event_day))::int AS grp
    FROM event_days
),
streaks AS (
    -- Group by the calculated group to identify consecutive day streaks
    SELECT 
        grp,
        COUNT(*) AS streak_count,
        MIN(event_day) AS start_date,
        MAX(event_day) AS end_date
    FROM numbered_days
    GROUP BY grp
)
-- Get the most recent streak
SELECT 
    streak_count,
    'April Meeting' AS event_name,
    'MST' AS timezone,
    start_date,
    end_date
FROM streaks
ORDER BY end_date DESC
LIMIT 1

WITH max_date_values AS (
    -- Get the got and gpt values at the maximum date for each patient
    SELECT l.id, l.date AS max_date, l.got AS max_got, l.gpt AS max_gpt
    FROM laboratory l
    INNER JOIN (
        SELECT id, MAX(date) AS date
        FROM laboratory
        GROUP BY id
    ) m ON l.id = m.id AND l.date = m.date
),
min_matching_date AS (
    -- Find the minimum date where got and gpt match the max date values
    SELECT l.id, MIN(l.date) AS min_date
    FROM laboratory l
    INNER JOIN max_date_values m ON l.id = m.id
    WHERE l.got = m.max_got AND l.gpt = m.max_gpt
    GROUP BY l.id
)
-- Get the full row details for the minimum matching date
SELECT l.id, l.date, l.got, l.gpt
FROM laboratory l
INNER JOIN min_matching_date m ON l.id = m.id AND l.date = m.min_date
ORDER BY l.id, l.date

WITH constructor_roles AS ( 
    SELECT 18 race_id, 1 constructor_id, false customer_billing, true commander, true agent 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT 18, 2, true, false, false 
),
-- Unpivot roles to separate rows so each true role becomes its own row
unpivoted_roles AS (
    SELECT race_id, constructor_id, 'AR (Customer Billing)' AS role
    FROM constructor_roles
    WHERE customer_billing = TRUE
    UNION ALL
    SELECT race_id, constructor_id, 'AP (Commander)' AS role
    FROM constructor_roles
    WHERE commander = TRUE
    UNION ALL
    SELECT race_id, constructor_id, 'AP (Agent)' AS role
    FROM constructor_roles
    WHERE agent = TRUE
)
SELECT 
    n.race_id, 
    string_agg(DISTINCT n.role, ', ') AS finance 
FROM unpivoted_roles n 
WHERE n.race_id = 18 
GROUP BY race_id

SELECT 
    s.firstName,
    jsonb_agg(
        jsonb_build_object(
            'yearId', year_classes.yearId,
            'classes', year_classes.classes
        )
    ) AS years
FROM users3 AS s
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT 
        cys.studentId,
        y.id AS yearId,
        jsonb_agg(jsonb_build_object('classId', c.id)) AS classes
    FROM classYearStudents AS cys
    JOIN classYears AS cy ON cy.id = cys.classYearId
    JOIN years AS y ON y.id = cy.yearId
    JOIN classes AS c ON c.id = cy.classId
    GROUP BY cys.studentId, y.id
) AS year_classes ON year_classes.studentId = s.id
GROUP BY s.id, s.firstName
ORDER BY s.id

WITH RECURSIVE ManagerChain AS (
    -- Base case: start with each employee as their own starting point
    SELECT 
        id as employee_id,
        salary as employee_salary,
        bossid,
        salary as boss_salary,
        0 as hierarchical_level
    FROM staff
    WHERE bossid IS NOT NULL  -- Only employees who have a boss
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: climb up the hierarchy
    SELECT 
        mc.employee_id,
        mc.employee_salary,
        s.bossid,
        s.salary as boss_salary,
        mc.hierarchical_level + 1
    FROM ManagerChain mc
    INNER JOIN staff s ON mc.bossid = s.id
    WHERE mc.bossid IS NOT NULL  -- Continue until we reach the top
),
QualifiedManagers AS (
    -- Find managers who earn at least twice as much as the employee
    SELECT 
        employee_id,
        employee_salary,
        hierarchical_level,
        bossid as boss_id,
        boss_salary,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY employee_id 
            ORDER BY hierarchical_level ASC
        ) as rn
    FROM ManagerChain
    WHERE boss_salary >= 2 * employee_salary
)
SELECT 
    employee_id as employeeId,
    employee_salary as employeeSalary,
    hierarchical_level,
    boss_id as bossId,
    boss_salary as bossSalary
FROM QualifiedManagers
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY employee_id

WITH RECURSIVE cte AS (
    -- Base case: start with the first row
    SELECT 
        id, 
        amount, 
        amount AS cum_sum, 
        1 AS bucket
    FROM transfer
    WHERE id = 1
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: join with next row
    SELECT 
        t.id,
        t.amount,
        CASE 
            WHEN c.cum_sum + t.amount > 20 THEN t.amount 
            ELSE c.cum_sum + t.amount 
        END AS cum_sum,
        CASE 
            WHEN c.cum_sum + t.amount > 20 THEN c.bucket + 1 
            ELSE c.bucket 
        END AS bucket
    FROM cte c
    JOIN transfer t ON t.id = c.id + 1
)
SELECT id, amount, cum_sum, bucket
FROM cte
ORDER BY id

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN t1.table_name IS NULL THEN 'extra'
        ELSE 'missing'
    END AS kind,
    COALESCE(t1.table_name, t2.table_name) AS table_name
FROM required_tables t1
FULL OUTER JOIN (
    SELECT table_name 
    FROM information_schema.tables 
    WHERE table_schema = 'public'
) t2 ON t1.table_name = t2.table_name
WHERE t1.table_name IS NULL OR t2.table_name IS NULL
ORDER BY kind, table_name

WITH 
old_data AS (
    SELECT id, stage FROM match WHERE id = 'match1'
),
updated_match AS (
    UPDATE match 
    SET stage = stage + 1 
    WHERE id = 'match1'
    RETURNING id, stage
),
updated_deps1 AS (
    UPDATE dependency d
    SET stage1 = (SELECT stage FROM updated_match)
    WHERE d.match1 = 'match1' AND d.stage1 = (SELECT stage FROM old_data)
),
updated_deps2 AS (
    UPDATE dependency d
    SET stage2 = (SELECT stage FROM updated_match)
    WHERE d.match2 = 'match1' AND d.stage2 = (SELECT stage FROM old_data)
)
SELECT * FROM updated_match

WITH hero_attribute(hero_id, attribute_id, attribute_value) AS ( 
    VALUES (1, 1, 80), (2, 1, 75), (3, 1, 95), (4, 1, 85), (5, 1, 90), (6, 1, 70) 
),
params AS (SELECT 2 AS n, 2 AS k, 2 AS kp),
bounds AS (
    SELECT MIN(hero_id) AS min_id, MAX(hero_id) AS max_id 
    FROM hero_attribute
),
anchors AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT ((hero_id - 1) / p.n) * p.n + 1 AS anchor
    FROM hero_attribute, params p
),
numbered_anchors AS (
    SELECT anchor, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY anchor) AS anum,
           COUNT(*) OVER () AS total_anchors
    FROM anchors
),
groups AS (
    SELECT 
        1 AS grp,
        GREATEST(b.min_id, a.anchor - p.k) AS start_id,
        LEAST(b.max_id, a.anchor + p.n - 1 + p.kp) AS end_id
    FROM numbered_anchors a
    CROSS JOIN params p
    CROSS JOIN bounds b
    WHERE a.anum = 1
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 
        2,
        GREATEST(b.min_id, (SELECT MIN(anchor) FROM numbered_anchors) - p.k),
        LEAST(b.max_id, (SELECT MAX(anchor) FROM numbered_anchors) + p.n - 1 + p.kp)
    FROM params p, bounds b
    WHERE (SELECT total_anchors FROM numbered_anchors LIMIT 1) > 1
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 
        3,
        GREATEST(b.min_id, a.anchor - p.k),
        LEAST(b.max_id, a.anchor + p.n - 1 + p.kp)
    FROM numbered_anchors a
    CROSS JOIN params p
    CROSS JOIN bounds b
    WHERE a.anum = a.total_anchors AND a.total_anchors > 1
)
SELECT SUM(ha.attribute_value) AS sum
FROM groups g
JOIN hero_attribute ha ON ha.hero_id BETWEEN g.start_id AND g.end_id
GROUP BY g.grp
ORDER BY g.grp

         FROM json_array_elements(skills) AS elem),
        ARRAY[]::text[]
    ) AS skills
FROM player_skills

     FROM json_array_elements(t.events) AS elem

FROM match_events as t

WITH pattern_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT("ANA Pattern") AS c, 
        "ANA Pattern", 
        EXTRACT(YEAR FROM "Examination Date") AS examination_year 
    FROM examination 
    WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM "Examination Date") BETWEEN 1993 AND 1996 
    GROUP BY EXTRACT(YEAR FROM "Examination Date"), "ANA Pattern"
),
max_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        examination_year, 
        MAX(c) AS max_c 
    FROM pattern_counts 
    GROUP BY examination_year
)
SELECT 
    pc.c, 
    pc."ANA Pattern", 
    pc.examination_year 
FROM pattern_counts pc
JOIN max_counts mc 
    ON pc.examination_year = mc.examination_year 
    AND pc.c = mc.max_c
ORDER BY pc.examination_year, pc.c DESC

WITH player_names AS (
    SELECT 
        1 AS source, 
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY player_name) AS row_number, 
        CONCAT(player_name, '(', LEFT(birthday, 4), ')') AS dest 
    FROM player_table
), 
birth_year_summary AS (
    SELECT 
        2 AS source, 
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY COUNT(*), LEFT(birthday, 4)) AS row_number, 
        CONCAT('There are a total of ', COUNT(*), ' player', CASE WHEN COUNT(*) > 1 THEN 's' ELSE '' END, ' born in ', LEFT(birthday, 4), '.') AS dest 
    FROM player_table 
    GROUP BY LEFT(birthday, 4)
) 
SELECT dest 
FROM (
    SELECT * FROM player_names 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT * FROM birth_year_summary
) AS combined_results 
ORDER BY source, row_number

SELECT 
    rs.raceId AS race_id, 
    res.constructor_ids, 
    p.stops 
FROM races rs 
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT raceId, string_agg(DISTINCT constructorId::TEXT, ',' ORDER BY constructorId::TEXT) AS constructor_ids 
    FROM results 
    GROUP BY raceId
) res ON res.raceId = rs.raceId 
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT raceId, string_agg(stop::TEXT, ', ' ORDER BY stop) AS stops 
    FROM pitstops 
    GROUP BY raceId
) p ON rs.raceId = p.raceId

SELECT r.id, r.start_day, r.end_day, de.point_of_delivery_plant_name, de.point_of_delivery_plant_number, de.visit_status 
FROM route r 
JOIN route_detail de ON de.route_id = r.id 
WHERE de.visit_status = '5'
AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 
    FROM route_event ev 
    WHERE ev.route_detail_id = de.id 
    AND ev.event_type = 3
)
ORDER BY r.id

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_team_attributes_eff_to() 
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$ 
BEGIN 
    -- Update the previous record for this team to end just before the new record's date
    UPDATE team_attributes 
    SET eff_to = TO_DATE(NEW.date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS') - 1
    WHERE team_api_id = NEW.team_api_id
      AND eff_to IS NULL
      AND id != NEW.id;
    
    -- Set the new record's eff_to to the far future date (will be updated when next record is inserted)
    NEW.eff_to := TO_DATE('5999-12-31', 'YYYY-MM-DD');
    
    RETURN NEW;
END; 
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

WITH ranked_drivers AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT position, driverid,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY position ORDER BY driverid DESC) as rn_desc,
           COUNT(DISTINCT driverid) OVER (PARTITION BY position) as driver_count
    FROM results
    WHERE position IS NOT NULL

SELECT driverid

WITH RECURSIVE 
max_depth AS (
    SELECT MAX(LENGTH(id) - 1) as max_d FROM superhero2
),
colored AS (
    SELECT s.id, s.parent_id, s.color, (LENGTH(s.id) - 1) as depth
    FROM superhero2 s, max_depth m
    WHERE (LENGTH(s.id) - 1) = m.max_d
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 
        p.id,
        p.parent_id,
        CASE 
            WHEN c2.id IS NULL THEN c1.color
            WHEN c1.color = c2.color THEN c1.color
            ELSE 'gray'
        END,
        p.depth
    FROM (
        SELECT id, parent_id, (LENGTH(id) - 1) as depth
        FROM superhero2
    ) p
    INNER JOIN colored c1 ON c1.parent_id = p.id
    LEFT JOIN colored c2 ON c2.parent_id = p.id AND c2.id > c1.id
    LEFT JOIN colored c3 ON c3.parent_id = p.id AND c3.id > c1.id AND c3.id < c2.id
    WHERE c3.id IS NULL
    AND p.depth = c1.depth - 1
)
UPDATE superhero2 
SET color = colored.color 
FROM colored 
WHERE superhero2.id = colored.id

WITH artist_set_counts AS (

        setcode,
        COUNT(*) AS cards_in_set,

    WHERE artist IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY artist, setcode
),
filtered_artists AS (
    SELECT *
    FROM artist_set_counts
    WHERE total_cards > 1
),
ranked_sets AS (
    SELECT 
        artist,
        setcode,
        cards_in_set,
        total_cards,
        RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY artist ORDER BY cards_in_set DESC) AS set_rank
    FROM filtered_artists
)
SELECT 
    artist,
    setcode,
    cards_in_set AS counter,
    CASE WHEN set_rank = 1 THEN 'Yes' ELSE 'No' END AS is_top_set
FROM ranked_sets
ORDER BY total_cards DESC, artist, cards_in_set DESC

WITH RECURSIVE chunks AS (
    -- Base case: all hero attributes with their original values
    SELECT 
        hero_id,
        attribute_id,
        attribute_value,
        CASE 
            WHEN attribute_value > 50 THEN 1 
            ELSE 0 
        END AS is_bifurcated,
        -- Calculate number of full 50 chunks needed
        CEIL(attribute_value::numeric / 50) AS total_chunks,
        -- Current chunk number (starts at 1)
        1 AS chunk_num
    FROM hero_attribute
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: generate additional rows for bifurcated attributes
    SELECT 
        hero_id,
        attribute_id,
        attribute_value,
        is_bifurcated,
        total_chunks,
        chunk_num + 1
    FROM chunks
    WHERE chunk_num < total_chunks AND is_bifurcated = 1
)
INSERT INTO hero_attribute_bifurcation (hero_id, attribute_id, attribute_chunk, is_bifurcated)
SELECT 
    hero_id,
    attribute_id,
    -- Calculate chunk value: 50 for full chunks, remainder for last chunk
    CASE 
        WHEN chunk_num < total_chunks THEN 50
        ELSE attribute_value - (chunk_num - 1) * 50
    END AS attribute_chunk,
    is_bifurcated
FROM chunks
ORDER BY hero_id, attribute_id, chunk_num

WITH ordered_activities AS (
    SELECT 
        ts,
        superhero_id,
        activity_code,
        LAG(ts) OVER (PARTITION BY superhero_id, activity_code ORDER BY ts) as prev_ts
    FROM superhero_activities
),
grouped AS (
    SELECT 
        ts,
        superhero_id,
        activity_code,
        CASE 
            WHEN prev_ts IS NULL OR ts - prev_ts > INTERVAL '5 minutes' 
            THEN 1 
            ELSE 0 
        END as is_new_group
    FROM ordered_activities
),
with_group_id AS (
    SELECT 
        ts,
        superhero_id,
        activity_code,
        SUM(is_new_group) OVER (PARTITION BY superhero_id, activity_code ORDER BY ts) as group_id
    FROM grouped
)
SELECT 
    superhero_id,
    activity_code,
    MIN(ts) as interval_start,
    MAX(ts) + INTERVAL '5 minutes' as interval_end,
    (MAX(ts) + INTERVAL '5 minutes') - MIN(ts) as total_duration
FROM with_group_id
GROUP BY superhero_id, activity_code, group_id
ORDER BY superhero_id, activity_code, interval_start

WITH RECURSIVE nodes AS (
    -- Base case: root nodes
    SELECT
        uuid,
        card_name,
        parent_uuid,
        ARRAY[uuid] AS path,
        0 AS depth
    FROM card_type
    WHERE parent_uuid IS NULL

    UNION ALL

    -- Recursive case
    SELECT
        c.uuid,
        c.card_name,
        c.parent_uuid,
        nodes.path || c.uuid,
        nodes.depth + 1
    FROM card_type c
    JOIN nodes ON nodes.uuid = c.parent_uuid
)
SELECT 
    uuid,
    card_name,
    parent_uuid,
    path AS hierarchy_path,
    depth AS level
FROM nodes
ORDER BY path

    SUM(CASE WHEN extract(year from trans.date) = 1995 THEN trans.amount ELSE 0 END) as "1995",
    SUM(CASE WHEN extract(year from trans.date) = 1996 THEN trans.amount ELSE 0 END) as "1996",
    SUM(CASE WHEN extract(year from trans.date) = 1997 THEN trans.amount ELSE 0 END) as "1997",
    SUM(CASE WHEN extract(year from trans.date) = 1998 THEN trans.amount ELSE 0 END) as "1998"

GROUP BY extract(month from trans.date)

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION words_get_chat(in_gid integer, in_social integer, in_sid text) RETURNS TABLE (out_mine integer, out_msg text) AS $func$ 
SELECT CASE WHEN c.uid = s.uid THEN 1 ELSE 0 END, c.msg 
FROM words_chat c 
JOIN words_games g USING (gid) 
JOIN words_social s ON s.uid IN (g.player1, g.player2) 
JOIN words_users u ON u.uid = c.uid
WHERE c.gid = in_gid 
  AND s.social = in_social 
  AND s.sid = in_sid 
  AND (u.muted = false OR c.uid = s.uid)
ORDER BY c.CREATED ASC; 
$func$ LANGUAGE sql

WITH RECURSIVE ordered_rows AS (
    SELECT id, trx, event, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY id) as rn
    FROM sales_transactions
),
recursive_calc AS (
    -- Anchor: first row
    SELECT 
        id, 
        trx, 
        event, 
        (trx * event)::numeric(10,6) as result,
        rn
    FROM ordered_rows
    WHERE rn = 1
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive: each result depends on previous calculated result
    SELECT 
        cur.id,
        cur.trx,
        cur.event,
        ((prev.result + cur.trx) * cur.event)::numeric(10,6),
        cur.rn
    FROM ordered_rows cur
    JOIN recursive_calc prev ON cur.rn = prev.rn + 1
)
SELECT id, round(result, 6) as result
FROM recursive_calc
ORDER BY id

WITH cte_funding_count AS (
    SELECT fundingtype, COUNT(*) AS count 
    FROM schools 
    GROUP BY fundingtype
), 
cte_total AS (
    SELECT SUM(count) AS total_count 
    FROM cte_funding_count
),
cte_with_raw_percent AS (
    SELECT 
        fundingtype, 
        count,
        count * 100.0 / total_count AS raw_percent
    FROM cte_funding_count
    CROSS JOIN cte_total
),
cte_with_floor AS (
    SELECT 
        fundingtype,
        count,
        FLOOR(raw_percent) AS floor_percent,
        raw_percent - FLOOR(raw_percent) AS decimal_part
    FROM cte_with_raw_percent
),
cte_ranked AS (
    SELECT 
        fundingtype,
        count,
        floor_percent,
        decimal_part,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY decimal_part DESC) AS rn
    FROM cte_with_floor
),
cte_adjusted AS (
    SELECT 
        fundingtype,
        count,
        floor_percent + CASE 
            WHEN rn <= (100 - (SELECT SUM(floor_percent) FROM cte_with_floor)) 
            THEN 1 
            ELSE 0 
        END AS percent
    FROM cte_ranked
)
SELECT fundingtype, count, percent
FROM cte_adjusted
ORDER BY percent DESC, fundingtype

WITH daily_first_prices AS (
    -- Get the first price of each card on each day
    SELECT DISTINCT ON (card_id, DATE(date_value))
        card_id,
        DATE(date_value) AS trade_date,
        date_value AS first_time,
        price AS first_price
    FROM card_prices
    ORDER BY card_id, DATE(date_value), date_value ASC
),
daily_last_in_15min AS (
    -- Get the last price within first 15 minutes (or last before 15 min mark)
    SELECT DISTINCT ON (card_id, DATE(date_value))
        card_id,
        DATE(date_value) AS trade_date,
        date_value AS last_time,
        price AS last_price
    FROM card_prices
    WHERE date_value < DATE(date_value) + INTERVAL '15 minutes'
    ORDER BY card_id, DATE(date_value), date_value DESC
),
price_differences AS (
    -- Calculate the difference for each card on each day
    SELECT 
        f.card_id,
        f.trade_date,
        f.first_price,
        l.last_price,
        l.last_price - f.first_price AS price_diff
    FROM daily_first_prices f
    JOIN daily_last_in_15min l 
        ON f.card_id = l.card_id 
        AND f.trade_date = l.trade_date
)
-- Calculate average difference for each card
SELECT 
    card_id,
    AVG(price_diff) AS avg_difference
FROM price_differences
GROUP BY card_id
ORDER BY card_id

WITH bond_atoms AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT bond_id, atom_id AS atom FROM connected
    UNION
    SELECT DISTINCT bond_id, atom_id2 AS atom FROM connected
)
SELECT DISTINCT
    b1.bond_id AS current_bond,
    b2.bond_id AS next_bond,
    b1.bond_type AS bond_type
FROM bond_atoms ba1
JOIN bond b1 ON ba1.bond_id = b1.bond_id
JOIN bond_atoms ba2 ON ba1.atom = ba2.atom AND ba1.bond_id != ba2.bond_id
JOIN bond b2 ON ba2.bond_id = b2.bond_id AND b1.molecule_id = b2.molecule_id
WHERE b1.bond_id < b2.bond_id
ORDER BY b1.bond_id, b2.bond_id

create or replace function setup_vector_store(table_name text) returns void language plpgsql as $$
declare
    match_func_name text;
begin
    -- Drop existing match function if it exists (using a fixed name or dynamic)
    execute format('drop function if exists match_%I(int, vector, jsonb)', table_name);
    
    -- Create the table with embedding column for vector storage
    execute format('create table if not exists %I (
        id bigint primary key generated always as identity,
        description text,
        metadata jsonb,
        embedding vector(1536)
    )', table_name);
    
    -- Create the match function with dynamic name to avoid conflicts
    execute format('create or replace function match_%I(
        match_count int default null,
        query_embedding vector(1536) default null,
        filter jsonb default ''{}''::jsonb
    ) returns table (
        id bigint,
        description text,
        metadata jsonb,
        similarity float
    ) language plpgsql as $func$
    begin
        return query
        select 
            t.id,
            t.description,
            t.metadata,
            1 - (t.embedding <=> query_embedding) as similarity
        from %I t
        where t.metadata @> filter
        order by t.embedding <=> query_embedding
        limit match_count;
    end;
    $func$;', table_name, table_name);
end;
$$

WITH 
-- Get all variable values with their variable names for each datapoint
datapoint_vars AS (
    SELECT 
        dpvv.datapoint_id,
        v.name AS variable_name,
        vv.name AS variable_value
    FROM datapoint_variablevalue dpvv
    JOIN variablevalue vv ON dpvv.variablevalue_id = vv.id
    JOIN variable v ON vv.variable_id = v.id
),
-- Pivot the variables into columns
datapoint_pivot AS (
    SELECT 
        datapoint_id,
        MAX(CASE WHEN variable_name = 'ProductType' THEN variable_value END) AS product_type,
        MAX(CASE WHEN variable_name = 'publishing_language' THEN variable_value END) AS language,
        MAX(CASE WHEN variable_name = 'genre' THEN variable_value END) AS genre
    FROM datapoint_vars
    GROUP BY datapoint_id
),
-- All distinct attribute combinations that exist
all_attributes AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT product_type, language, genre 
    FROM datapoint_pivot
    WHERE product_type IS NOT NULL 
       AND language IS NOT NULL 
       AND genre IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    COALESCE(SUM(dp.value), 0) AS total_sales,
    td.years,
    sd.name AS country,
    aa.product_type,
    aa.language,
    aa.genre
FROM time_dimension td
CROSS JOIN spatial_dimension sd
CROSS JOIN all_attributes aa
LEFT JOIN datapoints dp ON dp.time_dimension_id = td.id 
    AND dp.spatial_dimension_id = sd.id
LEFT JOIN datapoint_pivot dpp ON dp.id = dpp.datapoint_id 
    AND dpp.product_type = aa.product_type 
    AND dpp.language = aa.language 
    AND dpp.genre = aa.genre
GROUP BY td.years, sd.name, aa.product_type, aa.language, aa.genre
ORDER BY td.years DESC, sd.name, aa.product_type, aa.language, aa.genre

    mt.total_time,

WITH RECURSIVE monthly_periods AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        rate,
        LOWER(time_span)::DATE AS period_start,
        LEAST(
            (LOWER(time_span) + INTERVAL '1 month')::DATE,
            COALESCE(UPPER(time_span)::DATE, CURRENT_DATE)
        ) AS period_end_exclusive,
        COALESCE(UPPER(time_span)::DATE, CURRENT_DATE) AS max_date
    FROM budgets
    WHERE LOWER(time_span) IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 
        id,
        rate,
        period_end_exclusive,
        LEAST(
            (period_end_exclusive + INTERVAL '1 month')::DATE,
            max_date
        ),
        max_date
    FROM monthly_periods
    WHERE period_end_exclusive < max_date
)
SELECT 
    period_start AS start_date,
    (period_end_exclusive - 1) AS end_date,
    ROUND(rate::NUMERIC / NULLIF(period_end_exclusive - period_start, 0), 2) AS daily_rate
FROM monthly_periods
ORDER BY id, period_start

grouped_labs AS (

WITH top_counties AS (
    SELECT cname, AVG((avgscrread + avgscrmath + avgscrwrite) / 3.0) AS avg_total_sat 
    FROM satscores 
    WHERE avgscrread IS NOT NULL AND avgscrmath IS NOT NULL AND avgscrwrite IS NOT NULL
      AND avgscrread != 'nan' AND avgscrmath != 'nan' AND avgscrwrite != 'nan'
    GROUP BY cname 
    ORDER BY avg_total_sat DESC 
    LIMIT 5
)
SELECT s.cname AS county, s.sname AS school_name, 
       (s.avgscrread + s.avgscrmath + s.avgscrwrite) / 3.0 AS school_avg_sat
FROM satscores s
JOIN top_counties tc ON s.cname = tc.cname
WHERE s.avgscrread IS NOT NULL AND s.avgscrmath IS NOT NULL AND s.avgscrwrite IS NOT NULL
  AND s.avgscrread != 'nan' AND s.avgscrmath != 'nan' AND s.avgscrwrite != 'nan'
  AND s.rtype = 'S'
ORDER BY tc.avg_total_sat DESC, school_avg_sat DESC

    FROM curriculum_resources cr

) AS unnested

-- 1) Add a "status" column to curricula if not exists
ALTER TABLE curricula 
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS status TEXT DEFAULT 'installed';

-- 2) Recursive CTE to find all transitively dependent curricula
--    through shared educational resources
WITH RECURSIVE curricula_to_upgrade AS (
    -- Base case: the starting curriculum we want to upgrade
    SELECT c.curriculum_id
    FROM curricula c
    WHERE c.curriculum_id = 1   -- "Introduction to Programming"
      AND c.status = 'installed'

    UNION

    -- Recursive step: find curricula that share resources with already-found curricula
    -- We go: curriculum → curriculum_resources → resource_key → curriculum_resources → other curriculum
    SELECT cr2.curriculum_id
    FROM curricula_to_upgrade ctu
    -- Find all resources used by curricula in our set
    JOIN curriculum_resources cr1 ON cr1.curriculum_id = ctu.curriculum_id
    -- Find all other curricula that use the same resources
    JOIN curriculum_resources cr2 ON cr2.resource_key = cr1.resource_key
    -- Avoid re-processing already-found curricula to prevent cycles
    WHERE cr2.curriculum_id NOT IN (SELECT curriculum_id FROM curricula_to_upgrade)
)
UPDATE curricula
SET status = 'to upgrade'
WHERE curriculum_id IN (
    SELECT curriculum_id 
    FROM curricula_to_upgrade
)

WITH working_hours AS (
    -- Generate all working hour intervals (9:00-18:00) for each weekday in the range
    SELECT 
        generate_series(
            date_trunc('day', gs) + interval '9 hours',
            date_trunc('day', gs) + interval '17 hours',  -- 17:00 to get 9-18 slots (hour boundaries)
            interval '1 hour'
        ) AS work_hour_start
    FROM generate_series('2024-10-01'::date, '2024-10-09'::date, interval '1 day') AS gs
    WHERE EXTRACT(DOW FROM gs) BETWEEN 1 AND 5  -- Monday (1) to Friday (5)
),
working_intervals AS (
    -- Create proper 1-hour working intervals
    SELECT 
        work_hour_start,
        work_hour_start + interval '1 hour' AS work_hour_end
    FROM working_hours
),
overlaps AS (
    -- Find overlaps between events and working hours
    SELECT 
        e.learner_code,
        e.resource_key,
        -- Calculate actual overlap duration in seconds
        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM 
            LEAST(e.end_timestamp, w.work_hour_end) - 
            GREATEST(e.start_timestamp, w.work_hour_start)
        ) AS overlap_seconds
    FROM event_usage e
    JOIN working_intervals w
        ON e.start_timestamp < w.work_hour_end 
        AND e.end_timestamp > w.work_hour_start
    WHERE e.start_timestamp BETWEEN '2024-10-01' AND '2024-10-09'
)
SELECT 
    learner_code, 
    resource_key, 
    SUM(overlap_seconds) AS total_time_spent
FROM overlaps
WHERE overlap_seconds > 0
GROUP BY learner_code, resource_key

SELECT 
  learner_code,
  resource_key,
  usage_date,
  MAX(rating) OVER (
    PARTITION BY learner_code, resource_key, grp 
    ORDER BY usage_date
  ) AS filled_rating,
  rating AS original_rating
FROM (
  SELECT 
    learner_code,
    resource_key,
    usage_date,
    rating,
    SUM(CASE WHEN rating IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (
      PARTITION BY learner_code, resource_key 
      ORDER BY usage_date 
      ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND 1 PRECEDING
    ) AS grp
  FROM 
    resource_usage_2
) sub
ORDER BY 
  learner_code, resource_key, usage_date

SELECT 
    dd::date AS day_start,
    SUM(
        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM 
            LEAST(dd + interval '1 day', T.incident_end) -

        ) / 60
    )::int AS incident_minutes
FROM generate_series('2022-01-01'::timestamp, '2022-01-31'::timestamp, '1 day'::interval) dd
LEFT JOIN race_incidents T 
    ON dd < T.incident_end AND dd + interval '1 day' > T.incident_start
GROUP BY dd::date 
ORDER BY dd::date

    FROM patient p
    LEFT JOIN laboratory l ON p.id = l.id
)
SELECT

    MAX(CASE WHEN rn = 1 THEN ldh END) as ldh_1,

    MAX(CASE WHEN rn = 2 THEN lab_date END) as exam_date_2,
    MAX(CASE WHEN rn = 2 THEN ldh END) as ldh_2,

WITH month_params AS (
  SELECT 
    date_trunc('month', CURRENT_DATE)::date AS month_start,
    (date_trunc('month', CURRENT_DATE) + interval '1 month')::date AS month_end
),
week_calc AS (
  SELECT 
    l.date,
    m.month_start,
    -- First Sunday on or after month start (end of first administrative week)
    m.month_start + ((7 - EXTRACT(DOW FROM m.month_start)::int) % 7) AS first_sunday
  FROM laboratory l
  CROSS JOIN month_params m
  WHERE l.date >= m.month_start AND l.date < m.month_end
)
SELECT 
  CASE 
    WHEN date <= first_sunday THEN 1
    ELSE 1 + CEIL((date - first_sunday)::numeric / 7)::int
  END AS administrative_week,
  COUNT(*) AS record_count,
  MIN(date) AS week_start_date,
  MAX(date) AS week_end_date
FROM week_calc
GROUP BY 
  CASE 
    WHEN date <= first_sunday THEN 1
    ELSE 1 + CEIL((date - first_sunday)::numeric / 7)::int
  END
ORDER BY administrative_week

WITH filtered_budget AS (
    -- Filter for only Food and Advertisement categories
    SELECT 
        budget_id,
        category,
        amount,
        -- Assign priority: Food = 1 (higher priority), Advertisement = 2 (lower priority)
        CASE 
            WHEN category = 'Food' THEN 1
            WHEN category = 'Advertisement' THEN 2
            ELSE 3
        END AS priority
    FROM budget
    WHERE category IN ('Food', 'Advertisement')
),
ordered_budget AS (
    -- Order by priority first, then by amount (to maximize number of items, use smaller amounts first)
    SELECT 
        budget_id,
        category,
        amount,
        priority,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY priority, amount) AS item_order
    FROM filtered_budget
),
cumulative_calculation AS (
    -- Calculate cumulative sum across all items (not partitioned, to get running total of spending)
    SELECT 
        budget_id,
        category,
        amount,
        priority,
        item_order,
        SUM(amount) OVER (ORDER BY priority, amount ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS cum_amount
    FROM ordered_budget
)
SELECT 
    budget_id,
    category,
    amount,
    cum_amount,
    -- Show if this item can be purchased within the 500 budget
    CASE 
        WHEN cum_amount <= 500 THEN 'Purchased'
        ELSE 'Cannot Afford'
    END AS purchase_status,
    -- Show remaining budget after this purchase
    500 - cum_amount AS remaining_after_purchase
FROM cumulative_calculation
ORDER BY priority, amount

WITH district_1_clients AS (

other_district_clients AS (

    FROM client
    WHERE district_id <> 1
    LIMIT 1000
),
combined AS (
    SELECT client_id, district_id, gender, birth_date, 1 as priority
    FROM district_1_clients
    UNION ALL
    SELECT client_id, district_id, gender, birth_date, 2 as priority
    FROM other_district_clients
)
SELECT client_id, district_id, gender, birth_date
FROM combined
ORDER BY priority
LIMIT 1000

-- Step 1: Create loan table first (without the foreign key to card yet)
CREATE TABLE loan (
    loan_id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    amount DECIMAL(15,2) NOT NULL,
    duration INT NOT NULL,
    payments DECIMAL(15,2) NOT NULL,
    status TEXT NOT NULL,
    card_id INT UNIQUE
);

-- Step 2: Create card table with foreign key to loan with ON DELETE CASCADE
CREATE TABLE card (
    card_id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    loan_id INT UNIQUE,
    type TEXT NOT NULL,
    issued DATE NOT NULL,
    CONSTRAINT fk_card_loan FOREIGN KEY (loan_id) REFERENCES loan(loan_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
);

-- Step 3: Add foreign key from loan to card with ON DELETE SET NULL
-- This allows deleting a card without affecting the loan (just sets card_id to NULL)
ALTER TABLE loan 
ADD CONSTRAINT fk_loan_card FOREIGN KEY (card_id) REFERENCES card(card_id) ON DELETE SET NULL

WITH updated AS (
    UPDATE loan l
    SET status = 'updated_status',
        date = 'new_date',
        amount = 'new_amount'
    WHERE l.loan_id = 'loan_id_value'
    AND l.status = 'current_status'
    RETURNING l.loan_id
),
existing AS (
    SELECT loan_id FROM loan WHERE loan_id = 'loan_id_value'
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM updated) = 1 THEN 'successful update'
        WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM existing) = 0 THEN 'loan not found'
        ELSE 'version conflict'
    END AS outcome

UPDATE patients_info
SET general_info = jsonb_set(general_info, '{accept_appointments}', 'false'::jsonb)
WHERE id IN (
    -- Patients who have at least one appointment with hide_in_online_scheduling = TRUE
    SELECT patient_id
    FROM appointments
    WHERE hide_in_online_scheduling = TRUE
    -- And exclude patients who have any appointment with hide_in_online_scheduling = FALSE or NULL
    EXCEPT
    -- Patients who have any appointment with hide_in_online_scheduling = FALSE or NULL
    SELECT patient_id
    FROM appointments
    WHERE hide_in_online_scheduling IS DISTINCT FROM TRUE
)

WITH RECURSIVE 
-- First, enumerate all transactions with running totals
buy_enumerated AS (
    SELECT 
        transaction_id,
        molecule_id,
        date,
        molecules,
        usd_total,
        SUM(molecules) OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY date, transaction_id) AS cumulative_buy,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY date, transaction_id) AS rn
    FROM transaction
    WHERE sell = 0
),
sell_enumerated AS (
    SELECT 
        transaction_id,
        molecule_id,
        date,
        molecules,
        usd_total,
        SUM(molecules) OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY date, transaction_id) AS cumulative_sell,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY date, transaction_id) AS rn
    FROM transaction
    WHERE sell = 1
),
-- Create buy lots with their position ranges (start and end cumulative positions)
buy_lots AS (
    SELECT 
        molecule_id,
        date AS buy_date,
        molecules,
        usd_total,
        COALESCE(LAG(cumulative_buy) OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY rn), 0) AS start_pos,
        cumulative_buy AS end_pos,
        usd_total * 1.0 / molecules AS unit_price
    FROM buy_enumerated
),
-- Create sell lots with their position ranges
sell_lots AS (
    SELECT 
        molecule_id,
        date AS sell_date,
        molecules,
        usd_total,
        COALESCE(LAG(cumulative_sell) OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY rn), 0) AS start_pos,
        cumulative_sell AS end_pos,
        usd_total * 1.0 / molecules AS unit_price
    FROM sell_enumerated
),
-- Generate all possible (buy, sell) pairs where they overlap
overlaps AS (
    SELECT 
        b.molecule_id,
        b.buy_date,
        s.sell_date,
        b.start_pos AS buy_start,
        b.end_pos AS buy_end,
        s.start_pos AS sell_start,
        s.end_pos AS sell_end,
        b.unit_price AS buy_unit_price,
        s.unit_price AS sell_unit_price
    FROM buy_lots b
    JOIN sell_lots s ON b.molecule_id = s.molecule_id
    WHERE b.start_pos < s.end_pos AND b.end_pos > s.start_pos
),
-- Calculate the actual overlap amount for each pair
allocations AS (
    SELECT 
        molecule_id,
        buy_date,
        sell_date,
        -- Number of molecules in this allocation is the overlap of ranges
        LEAST(buy_end, sell_end) - GREATEST(buy_start, sell_start) AS molecules,
        buy_unit_price,
        sell_unit_price
    FROM overlaps
)
SELECT 
    molecule_id,
    buy_date,
    sell_date,
    molecules,
    ROUND(molecules * buy_unit_price, 2) AS buy_usd_total,
    ROUND(molecules * sell_unit_price, 2) AS sell_usd_total
FROM allocations
WHERE molecules > 0
ORDER BY molecule_id, buy_date, sell_date

WITH RECURSIVE weekdays AS (

),
dates AS (

    FROM weekdays w
),
schedule_blocks_with_dates AS (
    SELECT 
        sb.*, 
        d.date AS block_date
    FROM schedule_blocks sb
    JOIN dates d ON lower(sb.day_of_week) = lower(d.day_name)
    WHERE sb.is_available = TRUE 
      AND d.date >= CURRENT_DATE 
      AND d.date <= CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '2 months'
)
SELECT 
    block_id,

    block_date AS date, 
    start_time, 
    end_time
FROM schedule_blocks_with_dates
ORDER BY date, start_time

WITH RECURSIVE numbered_predictions AS (
    SELECT 
        prediction_code,
        Performance_Prediction,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY prediction_code ASC) as rn
    FROM performance_predictions
),
A(GroupNum, RollingSum, rn, Performance_Prediction) AS (
    SELECT 
        1, 
        Performance_Prediction, 
        rn,
        Performance_Prediction
    FROM numbered_predictions
    WHERE rn = 1
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN A.RollingSum + NP.Performance_Prediction > 200 
            THEN A.GroupNum + 1 
            ELSE A.GroupNum 
        END,
        CASE 
            WHEN A.RollingSum + NP.Performance_Prediction > 200
            THEN NP.Performance_Prediction 
            ELSE A.RollingSum + NP.Performance_Prediction 
        END,
        NP.rn,
        NP.Performance_Prediction
    FROM A
    JOIN numbered_predictions AS NP ON A.rn + 1 = NP.rn
)
SELECT 
    GroupNum,
    MAX(RollingSum) as cumulative_sum
FROM A
GROUP BY GroupNum
ORDER BY GroupNum

WITH attendance_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        link_to_member,
        link_to_event,
        COUNT(*) as attendance_count
    FROM attendance
    GROUP BY link_to_member, link_to_event
),
member_event_ranking AS (
    SELECT 
        link_to_member,
        link_to_event,
        attendance_count,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY link_to_member ORDER BY attendance_count DESC, link_to_event) as event_rank_for_member,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY link_to_event ORDER BY attendance_count DESC, link_to_member) as member_rank_for_event
    FROM attendance_counts
)
SELECT 
    link_to_member,
    link_to_event,
    attendance_count
FROM member_event_ranking
WHERE event_rank_for_member <= 2
   OR member_rank_for_event = 1
ORDER BY 
    link_to_member,
    event_rank_for_member

WITH ranked AS (
    SELECT 
        gasstationid,
        customerid,
        COUNT(*) as transaction_count,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY gasstationid ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC) as rn
    FROM transactions_1k
    GROUP BY gasstationid, customerid
)
SELECT gasstationid, customerid, transaction_count
FROM ranked
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY gasstationid

-- Step 1: Refresh backup table from source
TRUNCATE TABLE treatment_and_followup_backup;
INSERT INTO treatment_and_followup_backup SELECT * FROM treatment_and_followup;

-- Step 2: Merge source into target with delete for orphaned rows
MERGE INTO treatment_and_followup_latest AS t
USING treatment_and_followup AS s
ON t.patient_id = s.patient_id
WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET 
    treatment_followup_id = s.treatment_followup_id,
    followup_no = s.followup_no,
    primary_pathology_karnofsky_performance_score = s.primary_pathology_karnofsky_performance_score,
    primary_pathology_eastern_cancer_oncology_group = s.primary_pathology_eastern_cancer_oncology_group,
    primary_pathology_radiation_therapy = s.primary_pathology_radiation_therapy,
    primary_pathology_postoperative_rx_tx = s.primary_pathology_postoperative_rx_tx,
    has_follow_ups_information = s.has_follow_ups_information,
    has_drugs_information = s.has_drugs_information,
    has_radiations_information = s.has_radiations_information,
    project = s.project,
    day_of_form_completion = s.day_of_form_completion,
    month_of_form_completion = s.month_of_form_completion,
    year_of_form_completion = s.year_of_form_completion
WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT VALUES (
    s.treatment_followup_id, s.patient_id, s.followup_no,
    s.primary_pathology_karnofsky_performance_score, s.primary_pathology_eastern_cancer_oncology_group,
    s.primary_pathology_radiation_therapy, s.primary_pathology_postoperative_rx_tx,
    s.has_follow_ups_information, s.has_drugs_information, s.has_radiations_information,
    s.project, s.day_of_form_completion, s.month_of_form_completion, s.year_of_form_completion
)
WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE THEN DELETE

WITH src(id, geom) AS (
    VALUES 
        (1, 'linestring(0 0, 0 10)'::geometry), 
        (2, 'linestring(0 10, 0 11)'::geometry), 
        (3, 'linestring(1 1, 0 5)'::geometry), 
        (4, 'linestring(1 1, 2 2)'::geometry)
)
SELECT a.id, 
       b.id AS crossing_id,
       ST_AsText(ST_Intersection(a.geom, b.geom)) AS intersection_point
FROM src AS a
JOIN src AS b ON a.id < b.id
WHERE ST_Crosses(a.geom, b.geom)
   OR (
       ST_Touches(a.geom, b.geom)
       AND NOT ST_Equals(ST_StartPoint(a.geom), ST_Intersection(a.geom, b.geom))
       AND NOT ST_Equals(ST_EndPoint(a.geom), ST_Intersection(a.geom, b.geom))
   )

WITH params AS (
    SELECT 
        TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 10:34:36' AS start_ts,
        TIMESTAMP '2023-01-10 15:12:24' AS end_ts
),
total_interval AS (
    SELECT 
        start_ts,
        end_ts,
        end_ts - start_ts AS full_diff
    FROM params
),
weekend_seconds AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM d) > 5) * 86400 AS weekend_secs
    FROM params,
    LATERAL generate_series(DATE(start_ts), DATE(end_ts), INTERVAL '1 day') AS d
),
net_interval AS (
    SELECT 
        full_diff - (weekend_secs || ' seconds')::INTERVAL AS net_diff
    FROM total_interval, weekend_seconds
)
SELECT 
    EXTRACT(DAY FROM net_diff) || ' days ' ||
    EXTRACT(HOUR FROM net_diff) || ' hours ' ||
    EXTRACT(MINUTE FROM net_diff) || ' minutes ' ||
    ROUND(EXTRACT(SECOND FROM net_diff)::NUMERIC, 2) || ' seconds' AS time_diff
FROM net_interval

WITH links AS (
    SELECT 
        greatest(link.source_cluster, link.target_cluster) AS big, 
        least(link.source_cluster, link.target_cluster) AS smol, 
        link.type AS type 
    FROM link AS link
),
tag_pairs AS (
    SELECT 
        l.cluster AS left_id, 
        l.cluster_type AS left_type, 
        l.cluster_label AS left_label, 
        r.cluster AS right_id, 
        r.cluster_type AS right_type, 
        r.cluster_label AS right_label, 
        l.article
    FROM tag AS l
    JOIN tag AS r ON l.article = r.article AND l.cluster > r.cluster
)
SELECT 
    tp.left_id, 
    tp.left_type, 
    tp.left_label, 
    tp.right_id, 
    tp.right_type, 
    tp.right_label, 
    count(DISTINCT tp.article) AS articles, 
    array_agg(DISTINCT links.type) FILTER (WHERE links.type IS NOT NULL) AS link_types
FROM tag_pairs AS tp
LEFT JOIN links ON tp.left_id = links.big AND tp.right_id = links.smol
GROUP BY 
    tp.left_id, 
    tp.left_label, 
    tp.left_type, 
    tp.right_id, 
    tp.right_label, 
    tp.right_type
ORDER BY count(DISTINCT tp.article) DESC

WITH ranked_schools AS (

        opendate,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY county ORDER BY opendate) as rn

SELECT 
    county,
    MIN(CASE WHEN rn = 1 THEN opendate END) AS earliest_open_date,
    MIN(CASE WHEN rn = 4 THEN opendate END) AS fourth_open_date
FROM ranked_schools
GROUP BY county
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 4

    SELECT

        patient_id,
        primary_pathology_neoplasm_histologic_grade,

        primary_pathology_age_at_initial_pathologic_diagnosis,
        CASE 
            WHEN COALESCE(primary_pathology_initial_pathologic_diagnosis_method, 
                         primary_pathology_init_pathology_dx_method_other) 
                 LIKE 'Other method, specify:' 
            THEN TRUE 
            ELSE FALSE 
        END AS should_redact
    FROM pathology_and_surgery
)
SELECT 
    CASE WHEN should_redact THEN NULL ELSE primary_pathology_neoplasm_histologic_grade END AS histologic_grade,
    CASE WHEN should_redact THEN NULL ELSE primary_pathology_days_to_initial_pathologic_diagnosis END AS days_to_initial_diagnosis,

WITH ordered AS (
    SELECT 
        alb,
        art,
        feat_arts,
        start_date,
        end_date,
        CASE 
            WHEN start_date = LAG(COALESCE(end_date, '9999-12-31'::date) + INTERVAL '1 day') OVER (
                PARTITION BY alb, art, feat_arts 
                ORDER BY start_date
            ) THEN 0
            ELSE 1
        END AS is_new_group
    FROM sp_album_ctb
),
grouped AS (
    SELECT 
        alb,
        art,
        feat_arts,
        start_date,
        end_date,
        SUM(is_new_group) OVER (
            PARTITION BY alb, art, feat_arts 
            ORDER BY start_date
        ) AS grp
    FROM ordered
)
SELECT 
    alb,
    art,
    feat_arts,
    MIN(start_date) AS start_date,
    NULLIF(MAX(COALESCE(end_date, '9999-12-31'::date)), '9999-12-31'::date) AS end_date
FROM grouped
GROUP BY alb, art, feat_arts, grp
ORDER BY alb, art, start_date

WITH sales AS (

           "customerid" AS "CustomerId", 
           "date" AS "SaleDate", 
           "amount" AS "Amount" 
    FROM "transactions_1k" 
), 
initial_purchase AS ( 
    SELECT "GasstationId", 
           "CustomerId", 
           MIN(CAST("SaleDate" AS date)) AS "FirstPurchaseDate" 
    FROM sales 
    GROUP BY "GasstationId", "CustomerId" 
), 
customer_wages AS ( 
    SELECT i."CustomerId", 
           s.* 
    FROM sales s 
    JOIN initial_purchase i 
        ON s."CustomerId" = i."CustomerId" 
        AND s."GasstationId" = i."GasstationId"  -- Added missing join condition
    WHERE s."SaleDate" >= i."FirstPurchaseDate" 
    ORDER BY i."CustomerId" DESC 
)

DO $$
DECLARE
    rec RECORD;
BEGIN
    FOR rec IN 
        SELECT 
            quote_ident(c.table_schema) || '.' || quote_ident(c.table_name) AS full_table_name,
            quote_ident(c.column_name) AS old_column_name,
            quote_ident(lower(c.column_name)) AS new_column_name
        FROM information_schema.columns AS c 
        WHERE c.table_schema NOT IN ('information_schema', 'pg_catalog') 
          AND c.column_name <> lower(c.column_name)
        ORDER BY c.table_schema, c.table_name, c.column_name
    LOOP
        EXECUTE format('ALTER TABLE %s RENAME COLUMN %s TO %s', 
                       rec.full_table_name, 
                       rec.old_column_name, 
                       rec.new_column_name);
    END LOOP;
END $$
