WITH RankedPrices AS (
    SELECT 
        product_id,
        price,
        effective_from,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY product_id ORDER BY effective_from DESC) as price_rank
    FROM ProductPrice
    WHERE effective_to IS NOT NULL OR effective_to > NOW()
),
RecentPrices AS (
    SELECT 
        product_id,
        MAX(CASE WHEN price_rank = 1 THEN price END) as most_recent_price,
        MAX(CASE WHEN price_rank = 2 THEN price END) as previous_price
    FROM RankedPrices
    WHERE price_rank <= 2
    GROUP BY product_id
    HAVING COUNT(*) = 2
)
SELECT 
    p.product_id, 
    p.product_name
FROM Product p
JOIN RecentPrices rp ON p.product_id = rp.product_id
WHERE rp.most_recent_price < rp.previous_price
ORDER BY p.product_id

SELECT 
    CD.customer_id,
    COALESCE(S.total_sales, 0) AS total_sales,
    COALESCE(S.total_amount, 0) AS total_amount,
    COALESCE(C.total_communications, 0) AS total_communications,
    COALESCE(F.total_feedback, 0) AS total_feedback,
    COALESCE(F.total_positive_feedback, 0) AS total_positive_feedback,
    COALESCE(F.total_negative_feedback, 0) AS total_negative_feedback
FROM customer_details CD
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT customer_id, COUNT(*) AS total_sales, SUM(amount) AS total_amount
    FROM sales
    GROUP BY customer_id
) S ON CD.customer_id = S.customer_id
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT customer_id, COUNT(*) AS total_communications
    FROM communications
    GROUP BY customer_id
) C ON CD.customer_id = C.customer_id
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT 
        customer_id, 
        COUNT(*) AS total_feedback,
        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE feedback_type = 'positive') AS total_positive_feedback,
        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE feedback_type = 'negative') AS total_negative_feedback
    FROM feedback
    GROUP BY customer_id
) F ON CD.customer_id = F.customer_id

DELIMITER //

CREATE TRIGGER prevent_order_self_reference_before_insert
BEFORE INSERT ON Orders
FOR EACH ROW
BEGIN
    IF NEW.OrderID = NEW.OrderedByUserID THEN
        SIGNAL SQLSTATE '45000'
        SET MESSAGE_TEXT = 'Error: OrderID cannot be equal to OrderedByUserID (self-reference not allowed)';
    END IF;
END//

CREATE TRIGGER prevent_order_self_reference_before_update
BEFORE UPDATE ON Orders
FOR EACH ROW
BEGIN
    IF NEW.OrderID = NEW.OrderedByUserID THEN
        SIGNAL SQLSTATE '45000'
        SET MESSAGE_TEXT = 'Error: OrderID cannot be equal to OrderedByUserID (self-reference not allowed)';
    END IF;
END//

DELIMITER

SELECT 
    CONCAT_WS(', ',
        CASE WHEN HOUR(Description) > 0 THEN CONCAT(HOUR(Description), ' hour', IF(HOUR(Description) > 1, 's', '')) END,
        CASE WHEN MINUTE(Description) > 0 THEN CONCAT(MINUTE(Description), ' minute', IF(MINUTE(Description) > 1, 's', '')) END,
        CASE WHEN SECOND(Description) > 0 THEN CONCAT(SECOND(Description), ' second', IF(SECOND(Description) > 1, 's', '')) END
    ) AS formatted_duration
FROM backup_colour

CREATE TABLE `log_content` (
  `log_id` int(11) NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT,
  `content` varchar(32) NOT NULL,
  `no_of_chars` int(4) NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`log_id`)
);

DELIMITER //

CREATE TRIGGER `trg_log_content_insert`
BEFORE INSERT ON `log_content`
FOR EACH ROW
BEGIN
  SET NEW.`no_of_chars` = CHAR_LENGTH(NEW.`content`);
END //

CREATE TRIGGER `trg_log_content_update`
BEFORE UPDATE ON `log_content`
FOR EACH ROW
BEGIN
  SET NEW.`no_of_chars` = CHAR_LENGTH(NEW.`content`);
END //

WITH new_booking AS (
    SELECT '2011-12-19 12:00:00' AS new_start, '2011-12-19 15:00:00' AS new_end
),
time_points AS (
    SELECT d.start AS time_point, 1 AS delta
    FROM duration d
    JOIN new_booking nb ON d.start < nb.new_end AND d.end > nb.new_start
    UNION ALL
    SELECT d.end AS time_point, -1 AS delta
    FROM duration d
    JOIN new_booking nb ON d.start < nb.new_end AND d.end > nb.new_start
),
concurrent AS (
    SELECT 
        time_point,
        SUM(delta) OVER (ORDER BY time_point, delta DESC) AS concurrent_count
    FROM time_points
)
SELECT MAX(concurrent_count) AS max_overlaps
FROM concurrent

WITH categorized_sales AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN Score < 1000 THEN 'Under 1000' 
            WHEN Score BETWEEN 1000 AND 9999 THEN '1000 to 9999' 
            WHEN Score BETWEEN 10000 AND 49999 THEN '10000 to 49999' 
            WHEN Score >= 50000 THEN '50000 and above' 
            ELSE 'Unknown'
        END AS sales_range,
        Score AS amount_sold
    FROM posts
)
SELECT 
    sales_range,
    AVG(amount_sold) AS avg_amount,
    COUNT(*) AS transaction_count
FROM categorized_sales
GROUP BY sales_range
ORDER BY 
    CASE sales_range
        WHEN 'Under 1000' THEN 1
        WHEN '1000 to 9999' THEN 2
        WHEN '10000 to 49999' THEN 3
        WHEN '50000 and above' THEN 4
        ELSE 5
    END

RankedSkills AS (

SELECT 
    cr.release_id,
    cr.code_id,
    cr.code_status_id,
    cr.code_created_date
FROM card_release cr
INNER JOIN (
    -- For each code_id, determine which record to pick
    SELECT 
        code_id,
        CASE 
            -- If there's any paid status, pick the earliest paid record
            WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN code_status_id = 3 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1 
                THEN MIN(CASE WHEN code_status_id = 3 THEN release_id END)
            -- Otherwise, pick the most recent requesting record
            ELSE MAX(CASE WHEN code_status_id = 2 THEN release_id END)
        END as selected_release_id
    FROM card_release
    GROUP BY code_id
) selected ON cr.code_id = selected.code_id 
    AND cr.release_id = selected.selected_release_id

SELECT 
    e.event_id,
    e.event_name,
    COALESCE(expense_totals.total_expense, 0) AS total_expense,
    COALESCE(budget_totals.total_budget, 0) AS total_budget
FROM event e
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT 
        b.link_to_event,
        SUM(exp.cost) AS total_expense
    FROM budget b
    JOIN expense exp ON b.budget_id = exp.link_to_budget
    GROUP BY b.link_to_event
) expense_totals ON e.event_id = expense_totals.link_to_event
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT 
        link_to_event,
        SUM(amount) AS total_budget
    FROM budget
    GROUP BY link_to_event
) budget_totals ON e.event_id = budget_totals.link_to_event
WHERE expense_totals.link_to_event IS NOT NULL OR budget_totals.link_to_event IS NOT NULL

WITH date_range AS (
    SELECT '2012-08-23' AS dt
    UNION ALL SELECT '2012-08-24'
    UNION ALL SELECT '2012-08-25'
    UNION ALL SELECT '2012-08-26'
),
customer_dates AS (
    SELECT c.CustomerID, d.dt
    FROM customers c
    CROSS JOIN date_range d
),
daily_totals AS (
    SELECT 
        cd.CustomerID,
        cd.dt AS Date,
        COALESCE(SUM(t.Amount), 0) AS daily_amount,
        CASE 
            WHEN SUM(t.Amount) IS NULL THEN 'No transaction exists'
            ELSE CAST(SUM(t.Amount) AS CHAR)
        END AS daily_total_display
    FROM customer_dates cd
    LEFT JOIN transactions_1k t 
        ON cd.CustomerID = t.CustomerID 
        AND cd.dt = t.Date
    GROUP BY cd.CustomerID, cd.dt
)
SELECT 
    dt.CustomerID,
    dt.Date,
    dt.daily_total_display AS DailyTotal,
    SUM(dt.daily_amount) OVER (
        PARTITION BY dt.CustomerID 
        ORDER BY dt.Date 
        ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING
    ) AS RunningTotal
FROM daily_totals dt
ORDER BY dt.CustomerID, dt.Date

WITH RECURSIVE ContributionTree AS (
    -- Base: each node contributes its emission upward
    SELECT 
        entityid AS origin,
        entityid,
        parentid,
        emission,
        pctcontribution,
        CAST(emission AS DECIMAL(20,4)) AS cumulative_contribution
    FROM tree_table
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive: propagate contribution to parent with percentage weight
    SELECT 
        ct.origin,
        p.entityid,
        p.parentid,
        p.emission,
        p.pctcontribution,
        ct.cumulative_contribution * ct.pctcontribution / 100.0
    FROM ContributionTree ct
    JOIN tree_table p ON ct.parentid = p.entityid
)
SELECT 
    t.entityid,
    t.parentid,
    CAST(t.emission + COALESCE(SUM(ct.cumulative_contribution * ct.pctcontribution / 100.0), 0) AS DECIMAL(20,2)) AS cumulative_total
FROM tree_table t
LEFT JOIN ContributionTree ct ON ct.origin = t.entityid AND ct.entityid != t.entityid
GROUP BY t.entityid, t.parentid, t.emission
ORDER BY t.entityid

WITH AllIDs AS (
    SELECT id FROM Worker1 WHERE id > 0
    UNION
    SELECT id FROM Worker2 WHERE id > 0
),
Missing AS (
    SELECT 1 AS missing_id WHERE 1 NOT IN (SELECT id FROM AllIDs)
    UNION ALL
    SELECT a.id + 1 
    FROM AllIDs a
    LEFT JOIN AllIDs b ON a.id + 1 = b.id
    WHERE b.id IS NULL AND a.id + 1 > 0
)
SELECT MIN(missing_id) AS lowest_missing_value FROM Missing

    FROM TransactionResults 
    GROUP BY CustomerID 
    HAVING CustomerID = 3
)

INNER JOIN T0 ON CustomerTransactions.CustomerID = T0.CustomerID
SET

        CustomerID,
        COUNT(*) AS total_transactions,
        SUM(Amount) AS total_amount,
        SUM(Price) AS total_price
    FROM transactions_1k
    WHERE CustomerID IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY CustomerID
)
SELECT 
    CustomerID,
    total_transactions,
    total_amount,
    total_price,
    RANK() OVER (ORDER BY total_transactions DESC) AS transaction_rank,
    RANK() OVER (ORDER BY total_amount DESC) AS amount_rank,
    RANK() OVER (ORDER BY total_price DESC) AS price_rank
FROM CustomerStats
ORDER BY transaction_rank, amount_rank, price_rank

SELECT 
    first_flight.Team,
    first_flight.source,
    last_flight.destination
FROM 
    (SELECT Team, source, ID FROM source_destination_flight) first_flight
JOIN 
    (SELECT Team, MIN(ID) as min_id, MAX(ID) as max_id FROM source_destination_flight GROUP BY Team) bounds
    ON first_flight.Team = bounds.Team AND first_flight.ID = bounds.min_id
JOIN 
    (SELECT Team, destination, ID FROM source_destination_flight) last_flight
    ON last_flight.Team = bounds.Team AND last_flight.ID = bounds.max_id

SELECT 
    customer_daily_trips.c_id, 
    customer_daily_trips.trip_date AS date, 
    customer_daily_trips.trip_count,
    CASE 
        WHEN customer_daily_trips.trip_count = 1 THEN single.cost
        WHEN customer_daily_trips.trip_count = 2 THEN single.cost * 2
        WHEN customer_daily_trips.trip_count >= 3 THEN day_pass.cost
    END AS total_cost
FROM (
    SELECT 
        ct.c_id, 
        DATE(bt.start_time) AS trip_date, 
        COUNT(*) AS trip_count
    FROM CustomerTrip ct
    JOIN BusTrip bt ON ct.b_id = bt.b_id
    GROUP BY ct.c_id, DATE(bt.start_time)
) customer_daily_trips
CROSS JOIN (SELECT cost FROM TicketCosts WHERE duration = 'Single') AS single
CROSS JOIN (SELECT cost FROM TicketCosts WHERE duration = 'Day') AS day_pass

    FROM comments AS c 
    INNER JOIN users AS u ON c.UserId = u.Id

WITH consecutive_pairs AS (
    -- First, identify all consecutive purchase pairs
    SELECT 
        p1.product_name AS first_product,
        p2.product_name AS second_product
    FROM orders p1
    JOIN orders p2 
        ON p1.customer_id = p2.customer_id 
        AND p1.purchase_rank + 1 = p2.purchase_rank
),
pair_counts AS (
    -- Count occurrences of each specific pair
    SELECT 
        first_product,
        second_product,
        COUNT(*) AS pair_count
    FROM consecutive_pairs
    GROUP BY first_product, second_product
),
total_pairs AS (
    -- Calculate the grand total of all consecutive pairs
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS grand_total
    FROM consecutive_pairs
)
SELECT 
    pc.first_product,
    pc.second_product,
    pc.pair_count,
    ROUND(100.0 * pc.pair_count / tp.grand_total, 4) AS percentage_of_total
FROM pair_counts pc
CROSS JOIN total_pairs tp
ORDER BY pc.pair_count DESC

CREATE TABLE `content` (
  `id` int NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT,
  `item_id` int NOT NULL,
  `nested_item_id` int,
  `block_id` int,
  `order` int NOT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`id`),
  FOREIGN KEY (`item_id`) REFERENCES `cards`(`id`),
  FOREIGN KEY (`nested_item_id`) REFERENCES `cards`(`id`)
);

DELIMITER //

CREATE TRIGGER `prevent_self_parent` 
BEFORE INSERT ON `content`
FOR EACH ROW
BEGIN
  IF NEW.nested_item_id IS NOT NULL AND NEW.nested_item_id = NEW.id THEN
    SIGNAL SQLSTATE '45000' 
    SET MESSAGE_TEXT = 'A card cannot be its own parent';
  END IF;
END//

CREATE TRIGGER `prevent_self_parent_update` 
BEFORE UPDATE ON `content`
FOR EACH ROW
BEGIN
  IF NEW.nested_item_id IS NOT NULL AND NEW.nested_item_id = NEW.id THEN
    SIGNAL SQLSTATE '45000' 
    SET MESSAGE_TEXT = 'A card cannot be its own parent';
  END IF;
END//

-- Practical query: Get all transactions with gas station and product details
-- Plus summary of total amounts by date and product

SELECT 
    t1.Date,
    p.ProductID,
    p.Description AS ProductDescription,
    g.Country,
    g.Segment AS GasStationSegment,
    COUNT(t1.TransactionID) AS TransactionCount,
    SUM(t1.Amount) AS TotalAmount,
    SUM(t1.Price) AS TotalRevenue,
    AVG(t1.Amount) AS AvgAmount
FROM transactions_1k t1
LEFT JOIN gasstations g ON t1.GasStationID = g.GasStationID
LEFT JOIN products p ON t1.ProductID = p.ProductID
GROUP BY t1.Date, p.ProductID, p.Description, g.Country, g.Segment
ORDER BY t1.Date DESC, TotalRevenue DESC

SELECT 
    rating AS RATES,
    SUM(CASE WHEN A8 = rating THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS A8_COUNT,
    SUM(CASE WHEN A9 = rating THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS A9_COUNT
FROM district
CROSS JOIN (SELECT 5 AS rating UNION ALL SELECT 4 UNION ALL SELECT 3 UNION ALL SELECT 2 UNION ALL SELECT 1 UNION ALL SELECT 0) AS ratings
GROUP BY rating
ORDER BY rating DESC

SELECT
  u.Id,
  u.DisplayName,
  u.Age,
  (
    -- Count all days between CreationDate and LastAccessDate (inclusive)
    DATEDIFF(u.LastAccessDate, u.CreationDate) + 1
    -- Subtract weekends (Saturdays and Sundays)
    - (
      SELECT COUNT(*)
      FROM (
        SELECT DATE_ADD(u.CreationDate, INTERVAL n DAY) AS d
        FROM (
          SELECT a.N + b.N * 10 + c.N * 100 AS n
          FROM 
            (SELECT 0 AS N UNION SELECT 1 UNION SELECT 2 UNION SELECT 3 UNION SELECT 4 UNION SELECT 5 UNION SELECT 6 UNION SELECT 7 UNION SELECT 8 UNION SELECT 9) a,
            (SELECT 0 AS N UNION SELECT 1 UNION SELECT 2 UNION SELECT 3 UNION SELECT 4 UNION SELECT 5 UNION SELECT 6 UNION SELECT 7 UNION SELECT 8 UNION SELECT 9) b,
            (SELECT 0 AS N UNION SELECT 1 UNION SELECT 2 UNION SELECT 3 UNION SELECT 4 UNION SELECT 5 UNION SELECT 6 UNION SELECT 7 UNION SELECT 8 UNION SELECT 9) c
        ) numbers
        WHERE DATE_ADD(u.CreationDate, INTERVAL n DAY) <= u.LastAccessDate
          AND DATE_ADD(u.CreationDate, INTERVAL n DAY) BETWEEN '2010-01-01' AND '2014-12-31'
      ) dates
      WHERE DAYOFWEEK(d) IN (1, 7) -- Sunday = 1, Saturday = 7
    )
    -- Subtract holidays that fall on weekdays
    - (
      SELECT COUNT(*)
      FROM (
        SELECT '2010-01-01' AS holiday UNION ALL
        SELECT '2011-01-01' UNION ALL SELECT '2012-01-01' UNION ALL SELECT '2013-01-01' UNION ALL SELECT '2014-01-01' UNION ALL
        SELECT '2010-07-04' UNION ALL SELECT '2011-07-04' UNION ALL SELECT '2012-07-04' UNION ALL SELECT '2013-07-04' UNION ALL SELECT '2014-07-04' UNION ALL
        SELECT '2010-11-11' UNION ALL SELECT '2011-11-11' UNION ALL SELECT '2012-11-11' UNION ALL SELECT '2013-11-11' UNION ALL SELECT '2014-11-11' UNION ALL
        SELECT '2010-12-25' UNION ALL SELECT '2011-12-25' UNION ALL SELECT '2012-12-25' UNION ALL SELECT '2013-12-25' UNION ALL SELECT '2014-12-25'
      ) holidays
      WHERE holiday BETWEEN u.CreationDate AND u.LastAccessDate
        AND holiday BETWEEN '2010-01-01' AND '2014-12-31'
        AND DAYOFWEEK(holiday) NOT IN (1, 7) -- Only count if not on weekend (already excluded)
    )
  ) AS TotalWorkDays
FROM
  users u
WHERE
  u.LastAccessDate IS NOT NULL
  AND u.CreationDate IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY
  u.Id

SELECT 
    t1.CustomerID AS Customer1, 
    t2.CustomerID AS Customer2, 
    COUNT(DISTINCT t1.Date) AS CoOccurrenceCount
FROM 
    (SELECT DISTINCT CustomerID, Date, GasStationID FROM transactions_1k) t1
JOIN 
    (SELECT DISTINCT CustomerID, Date, GasStationID FROM transactions_1k) t2
    ON t1.Date = t2.Date 
    AND t1.GasStationID = t2.GasStationID
    AND t1.CustomerID < t2.CustomerID
GROUP BY 
    t1.CustomerID, 
    t2.CustomerID
ORDER BY 
    CoOccurrenceCount DESC

WITH DAILY_REVENUE AS (
    SELECT 
        t.Date, 
        SUM(t.Amount) AS TotalAmount
    FROM transactions_1k t
    JOIN gasstations g ON t.GasStationID = g.GasStationID
    WHERE g.ChainID = 13
    GROUP BY t.Date
),
MOVING_CALC AS (
    SELECT 
        Date,
        TotalAmount,
        SUM(TotalAmount) OVER (ORDER BY Date ASC ROWS BETWEEN 2 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS MovingSum,
        CAST(AVG(TotalAmount) OVER (ORDER BY Date ASC ROWS BETWEEN 2 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS DECIMAL(10,2)) AS MovingAvg,
        COUNT(*) OVER (ORDER BY Date ASC ROWS BETWEEN 2 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS FrameSize
    FROM DAILY_REVENUE
)
SELECT 
    Date,
    CASE WHEN FrameSize = 3 THEN MovingSum ELSE NULL END AS TotalAmount,
    CASE WHEN FrameSize = 3 THEN MovingAvg ELSE NULL END AS AverageAmount
FROM MOVING_CALC
ORDER BY Date

WITH RECURSIVE set_hierarchy AS (
    SELECT 
        code,
        parentCode,
        CAST(code AS CHAR(255)) AS ancestralCode
    FROM sets
    WHERE parentCode IS NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 
        s.code,
        s.parentCode,
        CONCAT(sh.ancestralCode, '-', s.code)
    FROM sets s
    INNER JOIN set_hierarchy sh ON s.parentCode = sh.code
)
SELECT 
    c.id,
    c.name,
    c.setCode,
    sh.ancestralCode
FROM cards c
JOIN set_hierarchy sh ON c.setCode = sh.code
WHERE sh.ancestralCode LIKE '%KHM%'

WITH event_attendance AS (
    -- Find the largest member ID who attended the event
    SELECT 
        link_to_event,
        MAX(link_to_member) AS largest_attendance_member
    FROM attendance
    WHERE link_to_event = 'recI43CzsZ0Q625ma'
    GROUP BY link_to_event
),
event_expenses AS (
    -- Find expenses for the event through budget, then find largest member with expenses
    SELECT 
        b.link_to_event,
        MAX(e.link_to_member) AS largest_expense_member,
        AVG(e.cost) AS avg_expense_cost
    FROM budget b
    JOIN expense e ON b.budget_id = e.link_to_budget
    WHERE b.link_to_event = 'recI43CzsZ0Q625ma'
    GROUP BY b.link_to_event
),
event_income AS (
    -- Find income linked to members, then find largest member with income for this event
    -- Income is linked to members, but we need to find which members attended this event
    SELECT 
        a.link_to_event,
        MAX(i.link_to_member) AS largest_income_member,
        AVG(i.amount) AS avg_income_amount
    FROM attendance a
    JOIN income i ON a.link_to_member = i.link_to_member
    WHERE a.link_to_event = 'recI43CzsZ0Q625ma'
    GROUP BY a.link_to_event
)
SELECT 
    e.event_id,
    e.event_name,
    ea.largest_attendance_member,
    ee.largest_expense_member,
    ee.avg_expense_cost,
    ei.largest_income_member,
    ei.avg_income_amount
FROM event e
LEFT JOIN event_attendance ea ON e.event_id = ea.link_to_event
LEFT JOIN event_expenses ee ON e.event_id = ee.link_to_event
LEFT JOIN event_income ei ON e.event_id = ei.link_to_event
WHERE e.event_id = 'recI43CzsZ0Q625ma'

SELECT 
    r.Segment,
    r.score AS result_score,
    s.value
FROM customer_results r
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT 
        s.*,
        LEAD(s.score) OVER (PARTITION BY s.Segment ORDER BY s.score) AS next_score
    FROM customer_scores s
) s ON s.Segment = r.Segment 
    AND r.score >= s.score 
    AND (r.score < s.next_score OR s.next_score IS NULL)

WITH Partitioned AS (
    SELECT *, 
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY CustomerID ORDER BY Date, Time) AS RowNumber 
    FROM transactions_1k
)
SELECT *,
    CASE 
        WHEN RowNumber = 1 THEN 0 
        ELSE COALESCE(
            DATEDIFF(
                a.Date,
                (SELECT MAX(Date) 
                 FROM transactions_1k 
                 WHERE Date < a.Date 
                   AND CustomerID = a.CustomerID)
            ), 0) 
    END AS Days_between 
FROM Partitioned a

    SELECT hero_id, GROUP_CONCAT(power_id ORDER BY power_id) as power_set

    GROUP BY hero_id
) h1
INNER JOIN (
    SELECT hero_id, GROUP_CONCAT(power_id ORDER BY power_id) as power_set
    FROM hero_power

) h2 ON h1.power_set = h2.power_set AND h1.hero_id < h2.hero_id

WITH max_cost AS (
    SELECT MAX(cost) as max_cost_value FROM expense
),
department_expense_counts AS (
    SELECT m.department, COUNT(e.expense_id) as expense_count
    FROM expense e
    INNER JOIN member mem ON e.link_to_member = mem.member_id
    INNER JOIN major m ON mem.link_to_major = m.major_id
    CROSS JOIN max_cost mc
    WHERE e.cost = mc.max_cost_value
    GROUP BY m.department
),
max_count AS (
    SELECT MAX(expense_count) as max_count FROM department_expense_counts
)
SELECT dec.department
FROM department_expense_counts dec
CROSS JOIN max_count mc
WHERE dec.expense_count = mc.max_count

SELECT 
    link_to_event,
    CASE 
        WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Closed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1 THEN 'Closed'
        WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1 THEN 'Open'
        WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1 THEN 'Planning'
        ELSE 'Unknown'
    END AS derived_status,
    CASE 
        WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Closed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1 
            THEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Closed' THEN remaining END)
        WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1 
            AND MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1
            THEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN spent END) 
               + MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN spent END)
        WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1 
            THEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN remaining END)
        WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1 
            THEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN remaining END)
        ELSE NULL
    END AS derived_remaining
FROM budget
GROUP BY link_to_event

WITH ranked AS (
    SELECT 
        m.season,
        m.league_id,
        DATE(m.date) as d,
        CAST(JULIANDAY(DATE(m.date)) AS INTEGER) - 
            ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY m.league_id, m.season ORDER BY DATE(m.date)) as grp
    FROM Match m
    WHERE m.league_id = 1
    GROUP BY m.league_id, m.season, DATE(m.date)
)
SELECT 
    season,
    league_id,
    MIN(d) as start_date,
    MAX(d) as end_date,
    COUNT(*) as consecutive_days
FROM ranked
GROUP BY season, league_id, grp
ORDER BY season, start_date

    s.School,

FROM

INNER JOIN

WITH ChangeHistory AS (
    SELECT
        s.CDSCode,
        s.School,
        s.FundingType,
        s.LastUpdate,
        DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY s.CDSCode ORDER BY s.LastUpdate) AS ChangeSequence,
        LAG(s.School) OVER (PARTITION BY s.CDSCode ORDER BY s.LastUpdate) AS PreviousSchool,
        LAG(s.FundingType) OVER (PARTITION BY s.CDSCode ORDER BY s.LastUpdate) AS PreviousFundingType
    FROM schools s
)

SELECT
    CDSCode,
    ColumnName,
    OldValue,
    NewValue
FROM (
    SELECT
        CDSCode,
        'School' AS ColumnName,
        ISNULL(PreviousSchool, '') AS OldValue,
        ISNULL(School, '') AS NewValue,
        ChangeSequence
    FROM ChangeHistory
    WHERE PreviousSchool IS NULL OR PreviousSchool <> School

    UNION ALL

    SELECT
        CDSCode,
        'FundingType' AS ColumnName,
        ISNULL(PreviousFundingType, '') AS OldValue,
        ISNULL(FundingType, '') AS NewValue,
        ChangeSequence
    FROM ChangeHistory
    WHERE PreviousFundingType IS NULL OR PreviousFundingType <> FundingType
) CombinedChanges
ORDER BY CDSCode, ColumnName, ChangeSequence

MERGE INTO account AS target
USING (VALUES 
    (4, 10, 'POPLATEK PO OBRATU', '2023-04-01'),
    (9999, 3, 'POPLATEK TYDNE', '2024-10-22')
) AS source (account_id, district_id, frequency, date)
ON target.account_id = source.account_id
WHEN MATCHED THEN
    UPDATE SET 
        target.frequency = source.frequency,
        target.date = source.date
WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN
    INSERT (account_id, district_id, frequency, date)
    VALUES (source.account_id, source.district_id, source.frequency, source.date)

    m.member_id,
    MAX(e.event_date) AS Latest_Event_Date
FROM member m
LEFT JOIN attendance a ON m.member_id = a.link_to_member
LEFT JOIN event e ON a.link_to_event = e.event_id
WHERE m.position <> 'Alumni'
GROUP BY m.member_id, m.first_name, m.last_name

WITH OrderedTrans AS (
    SELECT 
        trans_id,
        account_id,
        date,
        LAG(date) OVER (PARTITION BY account_id ORDER BY date, trans_id) AS prev_date
    FROM trans
),
Categorized AS (
    SELECT 
        trans_id,
        account_id,
        date,
        prev_date,
        CASE 
            WHEN prev_date IS NULL THEN 'New'  -- First transaction for account
            WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY, prev_date, date) <= 30 THEN 'Followup'
            ELSE 'New'  -- More than 30 days since previous, reset cycle
        END AS Category
    FROM OrderedTrans
)
SELECT TOP 10 
    trans_id,
    account_id,
    date,
    Category
FROM Categorized
ORDER BY trans_id

WITH member_pairs AS (
    -- Find all pairs of members who attended the same event
    SELECT 
        a1.link_to_member AS member1_id,
        a2.link_to_member AS member2_id,
        e.event_date
    FROM attendance a1
    JOIN attendance a2 
        ON a1.link_to_event = a2.link_to_event 
        AND a1.link_to_member < a2.link_to_member
    JOIN event e 
        ON a1.link_to_event = e.event_id
),
last_common_event AS (
    -- For each pair, find their most recent common event date
    SELECT 
        member1_id,
        member2_id,
        MAX(event_date) AS last_event_together
    FROM member_pairs
    GROUP BY member1_id, member2_id
),
oldest_pair AS (
    -- Find the pair with the oldest last common event
    SELECT TOP 1 
        member1_id,
        member2_id,
        last_event_together
    FROM last_common_event
    ORDER BY last_event_together ASC
)
-- Get member details for the pair
SELECT 
    m1.first_name + ' ' + m1.last_name AS member1_name,
    m1.email AS member1_email,
    m2.first_name + ' ' + m2.last_name AS member2_name,
    m2.email AS member2_email,
    op.last_event_together
FROM oldest_pair op
JOIN member m1 ON op.member1_id = m1.member_id
JOIN member m2 ON op.member2_id = m2.member_id

SELECT 
    DriverId, 
    ContractType,
    MIN(StartDate) AS StartDate, 
    MAX(EndDate) AS EndDate
FROM (
    SELECT *,
        SUM(CASE WHEN StartDate <= DATEADD(day, 1, PrevEndDate) THEN 0 ELSE 1 END) 
            OVER (PARTITION BY DriverId, ContractType ORDER BY StartDate) AS grp
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            DriverId, 
            StartDate, 
            EndDate,
            ContractType,
            LAG(EndDate) OVER (PARTITION BY DriverId, ContractType ORDER BY StartDate) AS PrevEndDate
        FROM driverContracts
    ) a
) b 
GROUP BY DriverId, ContractType, grp
ORDER BY DriverId, ContractType, StartDate

WITH RankedDates AS (
    SELECT 
        ID,
        Date,
        GOT,
        GPT,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ID ORDER BY Date DESC) as rn_desc,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ID ORDER BY Date ASC) as rn_asc,
        COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY ID) as cnt
    FROM Laboratory
),
LatestRecords AS (
    SELECT ID, Date
    FROM RankedDates
    WHERE rn_desc = 1 AND cnt >= 2
),
SecondLatestValues AS (
    SELECT ID, GOT, GPT
    FROM RankedDates
    WHERE rn_desc = 2
)
UPDATE L
SET 
    GOT = SLV.GOT,
    GPT = SLV.GPT
FROM Laboratory L
INNER JOIN LatestRecords LR 
    ON L.ID = LR.ID AND L.Date = LR.Date
INNER JOIN SecondLatestValues SLV 
    ON L.ID = SLV.ID

DECLARE @STARTDATE NVARCHAR(100) = '1995-04-04'
DECLARE @ENDDATE NVARCHAR(100) = '1995-04-05'


CREATE TABLE #TransTypes (
    TransType NVARCHAR(50)
)


INSERT INTO #TransTypes
SELECT DISTINCT [type]
FROM trans
WHERE [date] BETWEEN CAST(@STARTDATE AS DATE) AND CAST(@ENDDATE AS DATE)


DECLARE @cols NVARCHAR(MAX) = STUFF(
(
    SELECT DISTINCT
        ', ' + QUOTENAME(TransType)
    FROM #TransTypes
    FOR XML PATH(''), TYPE
).value('.', 'NVARCHAR(MAX)')
, 1, 2, '')


IF @cols IS NULL OR @cols = ''
BEGIN
    PRINT 'No transaction types found in the specified date range.'
    RETURN
END


DECLARE @sql NVARCHAR(MAX) =
N'
WITH DateRange AS (
    -- Generate all dates in the range to ensure all dates appear in output
    -- even if no transactions exist for that date
    SELECT CAST(''' + @STARTDATE + ''' AS DATE) + number AS FullDate
    FROM master.dbo.spt_values
    WHERE type = ''P'' 
    AND CAST(''' + @STARTDATE + ''' AS DATE) + number <= CAST(''' + @ENDDATE + ''' AS DATE)
)
SELECT dr.FullDate AS Date, ' +

STUFF((
    SELECT ', COALESCE(' + QUOTENAME(TransType) + ', 0) AS ' + QUOTENAME(TransType)
    FROM #TransTypes
    FOR XML PATH(''), TYPE
).value('.', 'NVARCHAR(MAX)'), 1, 2, '') +
N'
FROM DateRange dr
LEFT JOIN 
(
    SELECT 
        CONVERT(VARCHAR(10), [date], 120) AS Transaction_Date,
        [type],
        COUNT(*) AS n
    FROM trans
    WHERE [date] BETWEEN ''' + @STARTDATE + ''' AND ''' + @ENDDATE + '''
    GROUP BY CONVERT(VARCHAR(10), [date], 120), [type]
) s
PIVOT
(
    SUM(n)
    FOR [type] IN (' + @cols + ')
) pvt ON dr.FullDate = CAST(pvt.Transaction_Date AS DATE)
ORDER BY dr.FullDate;
'


DROP TABLE #TransTypes


EXEC sp_executesql @sql

DECLARE @info NVARCHAR(100) = '{"client_id":1,"gender":"F"}';
DECLARE @tempTable TABLE (id INT, jsonData NVARCHAR(MAX));

INSERT INTO @tempTable (id, jsonData)
VALUES (1, @info);

BEGIN TRY
    -- Check if valid JSON and gender field exists with value 'F'
    IF ISJSON((SELECT jsonData FROM @tempTable WHERE id = 1)) > 0
       AND JSON_VALUE((SELECT jsonData FROM @tempTable WHERE id = 1), '$.gender') = 'F'
    BEGIN
        -- Update the JSON in the table directly
        UPDATE @tempTable
        SET jsonData = JSON_MODIFY(jsonData, '$.gender', 'M')
        WHERE id = 1;
        
        -- Set output variable to the updated value
        SET @info = (SELECT jsonData FROM @tempTable WHERE id = 1);
    END
    ELSE IF ISJSON((SELECT jsonData FROM @tempTable WHERE id = 1)) = 0
    BEGIN
        SET @info = '{"error": "Invalid JSON"}';
    END
    ELSE
    BEGIN
        -- Valid JSON but gender is not 'F' or doesn't exist
        SET @info = (SELECT jsonData FROM @tempTable WHERE id = 1);
    END
END TRY
BEGIN CATCH
    SET @info = '{"error": "Invalid operation"}';
END CATCH

SELECT @info AS info

DECLARE @xml XML;

WITH SchemaInfo AS (
    SELECT
        COLUMN_NAME,
        DATA_TYPE,
        ORDINAL_POSITION
    FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS
    WHERE TABLE_NAME = 'schools' 
      AND TABLE_SCHEMA = 'dbo'  -- Use explicit schema or keep SCHEMA_NAME() if appropriate
),
SATData AS (
    SELECT
        cds,
        rtype,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY cds ORDER BY NumTstTakr DESC) AS RowRank
    FROM satscores
    WHERE NumTstTakr > 0
)

SELECT @xml = (
    SELECT
        (SELECT
            COLUMN_NAME AS '@Name',
            DATA_TYPE AS 'DataType'
         FROM SchemaInfo
         ORDER BY ORDINAL_POSITION
         FOR XML PATH('Column'), ROOT('Columns'), TYPE, ELEMENTS XSINIL),
        (SELECT
            cds AS 'CDS',
            rtype AS 'RecordType'
         FROM SATData
         WHERE RowRank = 1
         FOR XML PATH('Row'), ROOT('Rows'), TYPE, ELEMENTS XSINIL)
    FOR XML PATH(''), ROOT('Results')
);

-- Corrected final SELECT - use CAST to nvarchar(max) instead of .value() on sequences
SELECT 
    CAST(@xml.query('/Results/Rows/Row') AS nvarchar(max)) AS RowsXml,
    CAST(@xml.query('/Results/Columns/Column') AS nvarchar(max)) AS ColumnsXml,
    CAST(@xml AS nvarchar(max)) AS EntireXmlAsText

WITH RankedData AS (
    -- Convert Date to proper date format and add row numbers
    SELECT 
        CustomerID,
        Date,
        Consumption,
        -- Convert YYYYMM to a proper date for ordering
        DATEFROMPARTS(LEFT(Date, 4), RIGHT(Date, 2), 1) AS ProperDate,
        -- Row number within each customer ordered by date
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY CustomerID ORDER BY Date) AS rn
    FROM yearmonth
    WHERE Consumption = 0
),
GroupedPeriods AS (
    -- The key insight: consecutive dates will have the same (rn - month_number) value
    SELECT 
        CustomerID,
        Date,
        Consumption,
        ProperDate,
        -- This creates a unique group ID for each consecutive period
        DATEADD(month, -rn, ProperDate) AS PeriodGroup
    FROM RankedData
)
SELECT 
    CustomerID,
    -- Format back to YYYY-MM
    FORMAT(MIN(ProperDate), 'yyyy-MM') AS StartDate,
    FORMAT(MAX(ProperDate), 'yyyy-MM') AS EndDate,
    COUNT(*) AS MonthsInPeriod
FROM GroupedPeriods
GROUP BY CustomerID, PeriodGroup
ORDER BY CustomerID, StartDate

DECLARE @LabResults TABLE(
    ID INT,
    Date DATE,
    GOT INT,
    GPT INT,
    LDH INT
);

DECLARE @PatientInfo TABLE(
    ID INT,
    SEX NVARCHAR(10),
    Birthday DATE
);

-- First, get the examination dates with thrombosis to use for filtering
WITH ThrombosisExams AS (
    SELECT 
        ID,
        [Examination Date]
    FROM Examination
    WHERE Thrombosis = 1
)
INSERT INTO @LabResults
SELECT
    l.ID,
    l.Date,
    l.GOT,
    l.GPT,
    ISNULL(l.LDH, 
        AVG(l.LDH) OVER (PARTITION BY l.ID ORDER BY l.Date ROWS BETWEEN 1 PRECEDING AND 1 FOLLOWING)
    ) AS LDH
FROM Laboratory l
INNER JOIN ThrombosisExams e ON l.ID = e.ID 
    AND l.Date BETWEEN DATEADD(MONTH, -3, e.[Examination Date]) AND e.[Examination Date]
WHERE l.GOT IS NOT NULL 
    AND l.GPT IS NOT NULL;

INSERT INTO @PatientInfo
SELECT
    p.ID,
    p.SEX,
    p.Birthday
FROM Patient p
WHERE p.ID IN (SELECT ID FROM @LabResults)
    AND p.Birthday > '19500101';

SELECT
    lr.ID,
    pi.SEX,
    DATEDIFF(YEAR, pi.Birthday, lr.Date) AS Age,
    lr.GOT,
    lr.GPT,
    lr.LDH,
    CAST(lr.GOT AS FLOAT) / NULLIF(CAST(lr.GPT AS FLOAT), 0) AS Ratio
FROM @LabResults lr
LEFT JOIN @PatientInfo pi ON lr.ID = pi.ID
WHERE lr.GOT > (SELECT AVG(GOT) FROM @LabResults WHERE ID = lr.ID)
ORDER BY lr.Date

SELECT 
    date, 
    account_id, 
    CASE 
        WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN type IN ('PRIJEM', 'VYDAJ', 'SLUZBY') THEN type END) = 3 
        THEN 1 
        ELSE 0 
    END AS has_all_three_types
FROM trans 
GROUP BY date, account_id

WITH 
-- Merge overlapping blackout periods
blackout_merged AS (
    SELECT 
        MIN(start_date) as start_date,
        MAX(end_date) as end_date
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            start_date,
            end_date,
            SUM(CASE WHEN start_date > prev_end THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) 
                OVER (ORDER BY start_date) as grp
        FROM (
            SELECT 
                start_date,
                end_date,
                MAX(end_date) OVER (
                    ORDER BY start_date 
                    ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND 1 PRECEDING
                ) as prev_end
            FROM blackout_periods
        ) t
    ) t
    GROUP BY grp
),

-- Add sentinel boundaries and find gaps (non-blackout periods)
boundaries AS (
    SELECT CAST('1900-01-01' AS DATE) as start_date, CAST('1900-01-01' AS DATE) as end_date
    UNION ALL
    SELECT start_date, end_date FROM blackout_merged
    UNION ALL
    SELECT CAST('9999-12-31' AS DATE), CAST('9999-12-31' AS DATE)
),
non_blackout AS (
    SELECT 
        DATEADD(DAY, 1, LAG(end_date) OVER (ORDER BY start_date)) as start_date,
        DATEADD(DAY, -1, start_date) as end_date
    FROM boundaries
),
valid_gaps AS (
    SELECT start_date, end_date 
    FROM non_blackout 
    WHERE start_date <= end_date
),

-- Find intersection with active periods
result AS (
    SELECT 
        IIF(a.start_date > g.start_date, a.start_date, g.start_date) as start_date,
        IIF(a.end_date < g.end_date, a.end_date, g.end_date) as end_date
    FROM account_activity a
    INNER JOIN valid_gaps g
        ON a.start_date <= g.end_date AND g.start_date <= a.end_date
)

SELECT start_date, end_date
FROM result
WHERE start_date <= end_date
ORDER BY start_date

WITH ClientAccountSummary AS (

BabyBoomerFlag AS (

            ELSE 'No'
        END as is_baby_boomer

WITH ExpCTE AS
(
    SELECT
        DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY exp_id) AS ExpRN,
        exp_id,
        mid,
        qty
    FROM
        Experiment
    WHERE mid = 2
),
CTE AS
(
    -- Base case: first experiment
    SELECT
        e.exp_id,
        e.mid,
        e.qty,
        i.qty - e.qty AS QtyAvailable,
        CASE WHEN i.qty >= e.qty THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS Executable,
        e.ExpRN
    FROM
        ExpCTE e
    INNER JOIN
        Inventory i ON i.mid = e.mid
    WHERE
        e.ExpRN = 1

    UNION ALL

    -- Recursive case: subsequent experiments
    SELECT
        e.exp_id,
        e.mid,
        e.qty,
        CASE 
            WHEN c.Executable = 1 AND c.QtyAvailable >= e.qty THEN c.QtyAvailable - e.qty
            ELSE c.QtyAvailable
        END AS QtyAvailable,
        CASE 
            WHEN c.Executable = 1 AND c.QtyAvailable >= e.qty THEN 1
            ELSE 0
        END AS Executable,
        e.ExpRN
    FROM
        ExpCTE e
    INNER JOIN
        CTE c ON c.ExpRN + 1 = e.ExpRN
)
SELECT
    exp_id,
    mid,
    Executable AS excutable
FROM
    CTE
ORDER BY
    exp_id

IF OBJECT_ID('[dbo].[fnGetTeamAverageHeight]', 'FN') IS NOT NULL
    DROP FUNCTION [dbo].[fnGetTeamAverageHeight];
GO

CREATE FUNCTION [dbo].[fnGetTeamAverageHeight] (@team_api_id INT)
RETURNS FLOAT
AS
BEGIN
    DECLARE @averageHeight FLOAT;
    
    SELECT @averageHeight = AVG(P.height)
    FROM Player P
    WHERE P.player_api_id IN (
        SELECT DISTINCT home_player_1 FROM Match WHERE home_team_api_id = @team_api_id
        UNION
        SELECT DISTINCT home_player_2 FROM Match WHERE home_team_api_id = @team_api_id
        UNION
        SELECT DISTINCT home_player_3 FROM Match WHERE home_team_api_id = @team_api_id
        UNION
        SELECT DISTINCT home_player_4 FROM Match WHERE home_team_api_id = @team_api_id
        UNION
        SELECT DISTINCT home_player_5 FROM Match WHERE home_team_api_id = @team_api_id
        UNION
        SELECT DISTINCT home_player_6 FROM Match WHERE home_team_api_id = @team_api_id
        UNION
        SELECT DISTINCT home_player_7 FROM Match WHERE home_team_api_id = @team_api_id
        UNION
        SELECT DISTINCT home_player_8 FROM Match WHERE home_team_api_id = @team_api_id
        UNION
        SELECT DISTINCT home_player_9 FROM Match WHERE home_team_api_id = @team_api_id
        UNION
        SELECT DISTINCT home_player_10 FROM Match WHERE home_team_api_id = @team_api_id
        UNION
        SELECT DISTINCT home_player_11 FROM Match WHERE home_team_api_id = @team_api_id
        UNION
        SELECT DISTINCT away_player_1 FROM Match WHERE away_team_api_id = @team_api_id
        UNION
        SELECT DISTINCT away_player_2 FROM Match WHERE away_team_api_id = @team_api_id
        UNION
        SELECT DISTINCT away_player_3 FROM Match WHERE away_team_api_id = @team_api_id
        UNION
        SELECT DISTINCT away_player_4 FROM Match WHERE away_team_api_id = @team_api_id
        UNION
        SELECT DISTINCT away_player_5 FROM Match WHERE away_team_api_id = @team_api_id
        UNION
        SELECT DISTINCT away_player_6 FROM Match WHERE away_team_api_id = @team_api_id
        UNION
        SELECT DISTINCT away_player_7 FROM Match WHERE away_team_api_id = @team_api_id
        UNION
        SELECT DISTINCT away_player_8 FROM Match WHERE away_team_api_id = @team_api_id
        UNION
        SELECT DISTINCT away_player_9 FROM Match WHERE away_team_api_id = @team_api_id
        UNION
        SELECT DISTINCT away_player_10 FROM Match WHERE away_team_api_id = @team_api_id
        UNION
        SELECT DISTINCT away_player_11 FROM Match WHERE away_team_api_id = @team_api_id
    );
    
    RETURN @averageHeight;
END;
GO

DECLARE @StartDate datetime
DECLARE @EndDate datetime

SET @StartDate = '2010-02-21 23:00:00'
SET @EndDate = '2010-02-22 23:59:59'

SELECT *
FROM Team_Attributes
WHERE date BETWEEN @StartDate AND @EndDate

WITH vt AS (
    SELECT vt.*, 
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY CustomerID ORDER BY StartDate) AS seqnum
    FROM transactions_1k vt
),
cte AS (
    -- Base case: first transaction for each customer
    SELECT vt.TransactionID, 
           vt.CustomerID, 
           vt.StartDate, 
           vt.EndDate, 
           vt.StartDate AS last_unflagged_date,  -- Last unflagged transaction date
           0 AS flag,  -- First transaction is always unflagged
           vt.seqnum
    FROM vt
    WHERE seqnum = 1
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: compare current transaction to last_unflagged_date
    SELECT vt.TransactionID, 
           vt.CustomerID, 
           vt.StartDate, 
           vt.EndDate,
           -- Update last_unflagged_date only if current transaction is NOT flagged
           CASE 
               WHEN DATEDIFF(day, cte.last_unflagged_date, vt.StartDate) > 90 
               THEN vt.StartDate 
               ELSE cte.last_unflagged_date 
           END AS last_unflagged_date,
           -- Flag is 1 if within 90 days of last_unflagged_date, else 0
           CASE 
               WHEN DATEDIFF(day, cte.last_unflagged_date, vt.StartDate) <= 90 
               THEN 1 
               ELSE 0 
           END AS flag,
           vt.seqnum
    FROM cte 
    JOIN vt ON vt.seqnum = cte.seqnum + 1 
           AND vt.CustomerID = cte.CustomerID
)
SELECT cte.TransactionID, 
       cte.CustomerID, 
       cte.StartDate, 
       cte.EndDate,
       cte.flag
FROM cte
ORDER BY cte.CustomerID, cte.StartDate

WITH DistinctPayments AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT payments
    FROM loan
),
RankedPayments AS (
    SELECT 
        payments,
        DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY payments DESC) as rnk,
        COUNT(*) OVER () as total_distinct_count
    FROM DistinctPayments

SELECT

WITH status_changes AS (
    -- Identify when status 1 starts (either first row or status changed from non-1 to 1)
    SELECT 
        loan_id,
        date,
        status,
        CASE 
            WHEN status = 1 AND LAG(status) OVER (PARTITION BY loan_id ORDER BY date) <> 1 THEN 1
            ELSE 0
        END AS is_new_status_1
    FROM loan
),
status_1_occurrences AS (
    -- Create running count of status 1 occurrences
    SELECT 
        loan_id,
        date,
        status,
        SUM(is_new_status_1) OVER (PARTITION BY loan_id ORDER BY date) AS status_1_occurrence_count
    FROM status_changes
)
SELECT 
    loan_id,
    date,
    status,
    status_1_occurrence_count
FROM status_1_occurrences
WHERE loan_id = 10003
ORDER BY loan_id, date

WITH WindowedTrans AS (
    SELECT 
        DATEADD(MINUTE, DATEDIFF(MINUTE, 0, [date]) / 5 * 5, 0) AS clamped_time,
        [date],
        amount,
        balance,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY DATEADD(MINUTE, DATEDIFF(MINUTE, 0, [date]) / 5 * 5, 0) 
            ORDER BY [date]
        ) AS rn_asc
    FROM trans
),
FirstTrans AS (
    SELECT 
        clamped_time,
        amount AS open_amount
    FROM WindowedTrans
    WHERE rn_asc = 1
)
SELECT 
    f.clamped_time,
    f.open_amount AS [Open],
    MIN(w.balance) AS [Low]
FROM WindowedTrans w
JOIN FirstTrans f ON w.clamped_time = f.clamped_time
GROUP BY f.clamped_time, f.open_amount
ORDER BY f.clamped_time

-- Declare the input string and output variables
DECLARE @InputString nvarchar(MAX) = '1.25,5.34,6.9';
DECLARE @Var1 decimal(10,2), @Var2 decimal(10,2), @Var3 decimal(10,2);

-- Recursive CTE to split the string
WITH SplitCTE AS (
    -- Anchor: first value
    SELECT 
        CAST(LEFT(@InputString, CHARINDEX(',', @InputString + ',') - 1) AS nvarchar(MAX)) AS Value,
        STUFF(@InputString, 1, CHARINDEX(',', @InputString + ','), '') AS Remaining,
        1 AS Position
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive: subsequent values
    SELECT 
        CAST(LEFT(Remaining, CHARINDEX(',', Remaining + ',') - 1) AS nvarchar(MAX)),
        STUFF(Remaining, 1, CHARINDEX(',', Remaining + ','), ''),
        Position + 1
    FROM SplitCTE
    WHERE Remaining <> ''
)
SELECT 
    @Var1 = MAX(CASE WHEN Position = 1 THEN CAST(Value AS decimal(10,2)) END),
    @Var2 = MAX(CASE WHEN Position = 2 THEN CAST(Value AS decimal(10,2)) END),
    @Var3 = MAX(CASE WHEN Position = 3 THEN CAST(Value AS decimal(10,2)) END)
FROM SplitCTE;

-- Verify the results
SELECT @Var1 AS Var1, @Var2 AS Var2, @Var3 AS Var3

WITH SplitIDs AS
(
    SELECT
        pa.id,
        [xml_val] = CAST('<t>' + REPLACE(CAST(pa.player_api_id AS NVARCHAR(MAX)), ',', '</t><t>') + '</t>' AS XML)
    FROM Player_Attributes pa
    WHERE pa.player_api_id IS NOT NULL
),
ExplodedIDs AS
(
    SELECT 
        s.id,
        [player_api_id] = TRY_CAST(col.value('.', 'VARCHAR(100)') AS INT)
    FROM SplitIDs s
    CROSS APPLY s.[xml_val].nodes('/t') AS CA(col)
    WHERE TRY_CAST(col.value('.', 'VARCHAR(100)') AS INT) IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT TOP 10
    e.id,
    p.player_name,
    e.player_api_id
FROM ExplodedIDs e
INNER JOIN Player p
    ON e.player_api_id = p.player_api_id
